* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹Account.payouts(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsAccount#field-ShopifyPaymentsAccount.fields.payouts.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  Financial account information for merchants using Shopify Payments. Tracks current balances across all supported currencies, payout schedules, and [`ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransaction`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransaction) records.

  The account includes configuration details such as [`ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount) objects for receiving [`ShopifyPaymentsPayout`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout) transfers, statement descriptors that appear on customer credit card statements, and the [`ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSchedule`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSchedule) that determines when funds transfer to your bank. Access balance transactions to review individual charges, refunds, and adjustments that affect your account balance. Query payouts to track money movement between your Shopify Payments balance and bank accounts.

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹BankвЂ‹Account.payouts(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount#field-ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount.fields.payouts.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  A bank account that can receive payouts.

***

### Map

<a id="price-calculation-type"></a>

## PriceвЂ‹CalculationвЂ‹Type

enum

How to calculate the parent product variant's price while bulk updating variant relationships.

### Valid values

* COMPONENTS\_вЂ‹SUM

  The price of the parent will be the sum of the components price times their quantity.

* FIXED

  The price of the parent will be set to the price provided.

* NONE

  The price of the parent will not be adjusted.

## PriceвЂ‹ListвЂ‹FixedвЂ‹PricesвЂ‹ByвЂ‹ProductвЂ‹BulkвЂ‹UpdateвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `PriceListFixedPricesByProductBulkUpdateUserError`.

### Valid values

* DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹ID\_вЂ‹IN\_вЂ‹INPUT

  Duplicate ID in input.

* ID\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹MUTUALLY\_вЂ‹EXCLUSIVE

  IDs must be mutually exclusive across add or delete operations.

* LOCAL\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD\_вЂ‹ISSUANCE\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  The issuance currency of a local currency gift card must match the price list currency.

* LOCAL\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  The price of a local currency gift card cannot exceed the maximum gift card purchase limit.

* NO\_вЂ‹UPDATE\_вЂ‹OPERATIONS\_вЂ‹SPECIFIED

  No update operations specified.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  Exceeded the 10000 prices to add limit.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Price list does not exist.

* PRICES\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  The currency specified does not match the price list's currency.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product does not exist.

***

### Fields

<a id="price-list-price-origin-type"></a>

## PriceвЂ‹ListвЂ‹PriceвЂ‹OriginвЂ‹Type

enum

Represents the origin of a price, either fixed (defined on the price list) or relative (calculated using a price list adjustment configuration). For examples, refer to [PriceList](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/queries/priceList#section-examples).

### Valid values

* FIXED

  The price is defined on the price list.

* RELATIVE

  The price is relative to the adjustment type and value.

***

### Fields

* [PriceвЂ‹List.prices(originType)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceList#field-PriceList.fields.prices.arguments.originType)

  ARGUMENT

  A list that defines pricing for [product variants](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariant). Price lists override default product prices with either fixed prices or percentage-based adjustments.

  Each price list associates with a [`Catalog`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/Catalog) to determine which customers see the pricing. The catalog's context rules control when the price list applies, such as for specific markets, company locations, or apps.

  Learn how to [support different pricing models](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/markets/build-catalog).

* [PriceвЂ‹ListвЂ‹Price.originType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceListPrice#field-PriceListPrice.fields.originType)

  OBJECT

  Pricing for a [`ProductVariant`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariant) on a [`PriceList`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceList). Represents the variant's price, compare-at price, and whether the price is fixed or calculated using percentage-based adjustments. The [`PriceListPriceOriginType`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/PriceListPriceOriginType) distinguishes between prices set directly on the price list (fixed) and prices calculated using the price list's adjustment configuration (relative).

  Learn more about [building catalogs with different pricing models](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/markets/build-catalog).

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

* [PriceвЂ‹ListвЂ‹Price.originType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceListPrice#field-PriceListPrice.fields.originType)

#### Arguments with this enum

* [PriceвЂ‹List.prices(originType)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceList#field-PriceList.fields.prices.arguments.originType)

---

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `PriceListPriceUserError`.

### Valid values

* BLANK

  The input value is blank.

* LOCAL\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD\_вЂ‹ISSUANCE\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

* LOCAL\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  The price of a local currency gift card cannot exceed the maximum gift card purchase limit.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  The specified currency doesn't match the price list's currency.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  The price list doesn't exist.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FIXED

  Only fixed prices can be deleted.

* VARIANT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  A fixed price for the specified product variant doesn't exist.

***

<a id="price-list-user-error-code"></a>

## PriceвЂ‹ListвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `PriceListUserError`.

### Valid values

* BLANK

  The input value is blank.

* CATALOG\_вЂ‹CONTEXT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORT\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAKS

  Quantity price breaks can be associated only with company location catalogs or catalogs associated with compatible markets.

* CATALOG\_вЂ‹CONTEXT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORT\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULES

  Quantity rules can be associated only with company location catalogs or catalogs associated with compatible markets.

* CATALOG\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  The specified catalog does not exist.

* CATALOG\_вЂ‹MARKET\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  The price list currency must match the market catalog currency.

* CATALOG\_вЂ‹TAKEN

  Catalog has a price list already assigned.

* CONTEXT\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹OPTION

  Only one context rule option may be specified.

* COUNTRY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹ASSIGNMENT

  A country catalog cannot be assigned to a price list.

* CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MARKET\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  A price listвЂ™s currency must be the market currency.

* CURRENCY\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORTED

  The price list currency is not supported by the shop's payment gateway.

* GENERIC\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Something went wrong when trying to save the price list. Please try again.

* INCLUSION

  The input value isn't included in the list.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹MAX\_вЂ‹VALUE

  The adjustment value must not be greater than 1000% for `type` `PERCENTAGE_INCREASE`.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹MIN\_вЂ‹VALUE

  The adjustment value must not be greater than 100% for `type` `PERCENTAGE_DECREASE`.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹VALUE

  The adjustment value must be a positive value and not be greater than 100% for `type` `PERCENTAGE_DECREASE` and not be greater than 1000% for `type` `PERCENTAGE_INCREASE`.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹LOCKED

  The price list is currently being modified. Please try again later.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹PRIMARY\_вЂ‹MARKET

  Cannot create price list for a primary market.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  The specified price list doesn't exist.

* TAKEN

  The input value is already taken.

* TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  The input value is too long.

***

### Fields

* [PriceвЂ‹ListвЂ‹UserвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceListUserError#field-PriceListUserError.fields.code)

  Error codes for failed contextual pricing operations.

<a id="price-rule-allocation-method"></a>

## PriceвЂ‹RuleвЂ‹AllocationвЂ‹Method

enum

The method by which the price rule's value is allocated to its entitled items.

### Valid values

* ACROSS

  The value will be applied once across the entitled items.

* EACH

  The value will be applied to each of the entitled items.

***

### Fields

* [PriceвЂ‹Rule.allocationMethod](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceRule#field-PriceRule.fields.allocationMethod)

  OBJECT

  A set of conditions, including entitlements and prerequisites, that must be met for a discount code to apply.

  ***

  **Note:** Use the types and queries included our \<a href="https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/selling-strategies/discounts/getting-started">discount tutorials\</a> instead. These will replace the GraphQL Admin API\&#39;s \<a href="https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceRule">\<code>\<span class="PreventFireFoxApplyingGapToWBR">Price\<wbr/>Rule\</span>\</code>\</a> object and \<a href="https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/unions/DiscountCode">\<code>\<span class="PreventFireFoxApplyingGapToWBR">Discount\<wbr/>Code\</span>\</code>\</a> union, and the REST Admin API\&#39;s deprecated\<a href="https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-rest/unstable/resources/pricerule">\<code>\<span class="PreventFireFoxApplyingGapToWBR">Price\<wbr/>Rule\</span>\</code>\</a> resource.

  ***

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

* [PriceвЂ‹Rule.allocationMethod](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceRule#field-PriceRule.fields.allocationMethod)

---
<a id="price-rule-feature"></a>

## PriceвЂ‹RuleвЂ‹Feature

enum

The list of features that can be supported by a price rule.

### Valid values

* BULK

  The price rule supports bulk discounts.

* BUY\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹GET\_вЂ‹ONE

  The price rule supports Buy X, Get Y (BXGY) discounts.

* BUY\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹GET\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹WITH\_вЂ‹ALLOCATION\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  The price rule supports Buy X, Get Y (BXGY) discounts that specify a custom allocation limit.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹DISCOUNTS

  The price rule supports discounts that require a quantity.

* SPECIFIC\_вЂ‹CUSTOMERS

  The price rule targets specific customers.

***

* [PriceвЂ‹Rule.features](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceRule#field-PriceRule.fields.features)

  ***

* HOME

* PRODUCT

  The URL lands on a product page.

<a id="product-collection-sort-keys"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹CollectionвЂ‹SortвЂ‹Keys

enum

The set of valid sort keys for products belonging to a collection.

### Valid values

* BEST\_вЂ‹SELLING

  Sort by best selling.

* COLLECTION\_вЂ‹DEFAULT

  Sort by collection default order.

* CREATED

  Sort by creation time.

* ID

  Sort by id.

* MANUAL

  Sort by manual order.

* PRICE

  Sort by price.

* RELEVANCE

  Sort by relevance.

* TITLE

  Sort by title.

***

### Fields

* [Collection.products(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Collection#field-Collection.fields.products.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The `Collection` object represents a group of [products](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product) that merchants can organize to make their stores easier to browse and help customers find related products. Collections serve as the primary way to categorize and display products across [online stores](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/online-store), [sales channels](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/sales-channels), and marketing campaigns.

  There are two types of collections:

  * **[Custom (manual) collections](https://help.shopify.com/manual/products/collections/manual-shopify-collection)**: You specify the products to include in a collection.
  * **[Smart (automated) collections](https://help.shopify.com/manual/products/collections/automated-collections)**: You define rules, and products matching those rules are automatically included in the collection.

  The `Collection` object provides information to:

  * Organize products by category, season, or promotion.
  * Automate product grouping using rules (for example, by tag, type, or price).
  * Configure product sorting and display order (for example, alphabetical, best-selling, price, or manual).
  * Manage collection visibility and publication across sales channels.
  * Add rich descriptions, images, and metadata to enhance discovery.

  ***

  **Note:** Collections are unpublished by default. To make them available to customers, use the \<a href="https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/publishablePublish">\<code>\<span class="PreventFireFoxApplyingGapToWBR">publishable\<wbr/>Publish\</span>\</code>\</a> mutation after creation.

  ***

  Collections can be displayed in a store with Shopify's theme system through [Liquid templates](https://shopify.dev/docs/storefronts/themes/architecture/templates/collection) and can be customized with [template suffixes](https://shopify.dev/docs/storefronts/themes/architecture/templates/alternate-templates) for unique layouts. They also support advanced features like translated content, resource feedback, and contextual publication for location-based catalogs.

  Learn about [using metafields with smart collections](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/custom-data/metafields/use-metafield-capabilities).

***

### Map

#### Arguments with this enum

* [Collection.products(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Collection#field-Collection.fields.products.arguments.sortKey)

---

## ProductвЂ‹ImageвЂ‹SortвЂ‹Keys

enum

The set of valid sort keys for the ProductImage query.

### Valid values

* CREATED\_вЂ‹AT

  Sort by the `created_at` value.

* ID

  Sort by the `id` value.

* POSITION

  Sort by the `position` value.

***

### Fields

* [Product.images(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product#field-Product.fields.images.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The `Product` object lets you manage products in a merchantвЂ™s store.

  Products are the goods and services that merchants offer to customers. They can include various details such as title, description, price, images, and options such as size or color. You can use [product variants](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/productvariant) to create or update different versions of the same product. You can also add or update product [media](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/media). Products can be organized by grouping them into a [collection](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/collection).

  Learn more about working with [Shopify's product model](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/graphql/migrate/new-product-model/product-model-components), including limitations and considerations.

* [Shop.productImages(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Shop#field-Shop.fields.productImages.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The central configuration and settings hub for a Shopify store. Access business information, operational preferences, feature availability, and store-wide settings that control how the shop operates.

  Includes core business details like the shop name, contact emails, billing address, and currency settings. The shop configuration determines customer account requirements, available sales channels, enabled features, payment settings, and policy documents. Also provides access to shop-level resources such as staff members, fulfillment services, navigation settings, and storefront access tokens.

***

### Map

#### Arguments with this enum

* [Product.images(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product#field-Product.fields.images.arguments.sortKey)
* [Shop.productImages(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Shop#field-Shop.fields.productImages.arguments.sortKey)

<a id="product-operation-status"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹OperationвЂ‹Status

enum

Represents the state of this product operation.

### Valid values

* ACTIVE

  Operation is currently running.

* COMPLETE

  Operation is complete.

* CREATED

  Operation has been created.

***

### Fields

* [ProductвЂ‹BundleвЂ‹Operation.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductBundleOperation#field-ProductBundleOperation.fields.status)

  OBJECT

  An entity that represents details of an asynchronous [ProductBundleCreate](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/mutations/productBundleCreate) or [ProductBundleUpdate](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/mutations/productBundleUpdate) mutation.

  By querying this entity with the [productOperation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/queries/productOperation) query using the ID that was returned when the bundle was created or updated, this can be used to check the status of an operation.

  The `status` field indicates whether the operation is `CREATED`, `ACTIVE`, or `COMPLETE`.

  The `product` field provides the details of the created or updated product.

  The `userErrors` field provides mutation errors that occurred during the operation.

* [ProductвЂ‹DeleteвЂ‹Operation.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductDeleteOperation#field-ProductDeleteOperation.fields.status)

  OBJECT

  An entity that represents details of an asynchronous [ProductDelete](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/mutations/productDelete) mutation.

  By querying this entity with the [productOperation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/queries/productOperation) query using the ID that was returned when the product was deleted, this can be used to check the status of an operation.

  The `status` field indicates whether the operation is `CREATED`, `ACTIVE`, or `COMPLETE`.

  The `deletedProductId` field provides the ID of the deleted product.

  The `userErrors` field provides mutation errors that occurred during the operation.

* [ProductвЂ‹DuplicateвЂ‹Operation.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductDuplicateOperation#field-ProductDuplicateOperation.fields.status)

  OBJECT

  An entity that represents details of an asynchronous [ProductDuplicate](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/mutations/productDuplicate) mutation.

  By querying this entity with the [productOperation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/queries/productOperation) query using the ID that was returned [when the product was duplicated](https://shopify.dev/api/admin/migrate/new-product-model/sync-data#create-a-product-with-variants-and-options-asynchronously), this can be used to check the status of an operation.

  The `status` field indicates whether the operation is `CREATED`, `ACTIVE`, or `COMPLETE`.

  The `product` field provides the details of the original product.

  The `newProduct` field provides the details of the new duplicate of the product.

  The `userErrors` field provides mutation errors that occurred during the operation.

* [ProductвЂ‹Operation.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/ProductOperation#fields-status)

  INTERFACE

  An interface representing asynchronous operations on products. Tracks the status and details of background product mutations like `productSet`, `productDelete`, `productDuplicate`, and `productBundle` operations. Provides status field (CREATED, ACTIVE, COMPLETE) and product field to monitor long-running product operations.

* [ProductвЂ‹SetвЂ‹Operation.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductSetOperation#field-ProductSetOperation.fields.status)

  OBJECT

  An entity that represents details of an asynchronous [ProductSet](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/mutations/productSet) mutation.

  By querying this entity with the [productOperation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/current/queries/productOperation) query using the ID that was returned [when the product was created or updated](https://shopify.dev/api/admin/migrate/new-product-model/sync-data#create-a-product-with-variants-and-options-asynchronously), this can be used to check the status of an operation.

  The `status` field indicates whether the operation is `CREATED`, `ACTIVE`, or `COMPLETE`.

  The `product` field provides the details of the created or updated product.

  The `userErrors` field provides mutation errors that occurred during the operation.

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

* [ProductвЂ‹BundleвЂ‹Operation.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductBundleOperation#field-ProductBundleOperation.fields.status)
* [ProductвЂ‹DeleteвЂ‹Operation.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductDeleteOperation#field-ProductDeleteOperation.fields.status)
* [ProductвЂ‹DuplicateвЂ‹Operation.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductDuplicateOperation#field-ProductDuplicateOperation.fields.status)

<a id="product-options-create-user-error-code"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹OptionsвЂ‹CreateвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `ProductOptionsCreateUserError`.

### Valid values

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹COMBINE\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES

  Cannot combine linked metafield and option values.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹MAKE\_вЂ‹CHANGES\_вЂ‹IF\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹MISSING\_вЂ‹REQUIRED\_вЂ‹SKU

  At least one of the product variants has invalid SKUs.

* DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION

  Cannot link multiple options to the same metafield.

* DUPLICATED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹NAME

  Duplicated option name.

* DUPLICATED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Duplicated option value.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION

  Invalid metafield value for linked option.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹NAME

  The name provided is not valid.

* LINKED\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹DEFINITION\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  No valid metafield definition found for linked option.

* LINKED\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹WITHOUT\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION

  Cannot specify 'linkedMetafieldValue' for an option that is not linked to a metafield.

* LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTIONS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORTED\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹SHOP

  Linked options are currently not supported for this shop.

* MISSING\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹VALUES\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION

  Missing metafield values for linked option.

* NEW\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹WITHOUT\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹EXISTING\_вЂ‹VARIANTS

  Cannot create new options without values for all existing variants.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹EXISTS

  Option already exists.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹TAKEN

  An option linked to the provided metafield already exists.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹NAME\_вЂ‹MISSING

  Each option must have a name specified.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹NAME\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  Option name is too long.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹POSITION\_вЂ‹MISSING

  If specified, position field must be present in all option inputs.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹NAME\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  Option value name is too long.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES\_вЂ‹MISSING

  Each option must have at least one option value specified.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES\_вЂ‹OVER\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  Option values count is over the allowed limit.

* OPTIONS\_вЂ‹OVER\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  Options count is over the allowed limit.

* POSITION\_вЂ‹OUT\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹BOUNDS

  Position must be between 1 and the maximum number of options per product.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹SUSPENDED

  Product is suspended.

* TOO\_вЂ‹MANY\_вЂ‹VARIANTS\_вЂ‹CREATED

  The number of option values created with the CREATE strategy would exceed the variant limit.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹COMBINED\_вЂ‹LISTING\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹OPERATION

  Operation is not supported for a combined listing parent product.

<a id="product-option-create-variant-strategy"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹OptionвЂ‹CreateвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹Strategy

enum

Requires `read_products` access scope.

The set of variant strategies available for use in the `productOptionsCreate` mutation.

### Valid values

* CREATE

  Existing variants are updated with the first option value of each added option. New variants are created for each combination of existing variant option values and new option values.

* LEAVE\_вЂ‹AS\_вЂ‹IS

  No additional variants are created in response to the added options. Existing variants are updated with the first option value of each option added.

***

### Fields

* [productвЂ‹OptionsвЂ‹Create.variantStrategy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productOptionsCreate#arguments-variantStrategy)

  ARGUMENT

***

### Map

#### Arguments with this enum

* [productвЂ‹OptionsвЂ‹Create.variantStrategy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productOptionsCreate#arguments-variantStrategy)

---
<a id="product-option-delete-strategy"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹OptionвЂ‹DeleteвЂ‹Strategy

enum

Requires `read_products` access scope.

The set of strategies available for use on the `productOptionDelete` mutation.

### Valid values

* DEFAULT

  The default strategy, the specified `Option` may only have one corresponding `value`.

* NON\_вЂ‹DESTRUCTIVE

  An `Option` with multiple `values` can be deleted, but the operation only succeeds if no product variants get deleted.

* POSITION

  An `Option` with multiple `values` can be deleted. Remaining variants will be deleted, highest `position` first, in the event of duplicates being detected.

***

### Fields

* [productвЂ‹OptionsвЂ‹Delete.strategy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productOptionsDelete#arguments-strategy)

  ARGUMENT

***

### Map

* [productвЂ‹OptionsвЂ‹Delete.strategy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productOptionsDelete#arguments-strategy)

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `ProductOptionUpdateUserError`.

### Valid values

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹COMBINE\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹NONLINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES

  An option cannot have both metafield linked and nonlinked option values.

  Deleting all option values of an option is not allowed.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹DELETE\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹WITHOUT\_вЂ‹PERMISSION

  Cannot update the option because it would result in deleting variants, and you don't have the required permissions.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹LEAVE\_вЂ‹OPTIONS\_вЂ‹WITHOUT\_вЂ‹VARIANTS

  An option cannot be left only with option values that are not linked to any variant.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹MAKE\_вЂ‹CHANGES\_вЂ‹IF\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹MISSING\_вЂ‹REQUIRED\_вЂ‹SKU

  At least one of the product variants has invalid SKUs.

* DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION

  Cannot link multiple options to the same metafield.

* DUPLICATED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Duplicated option value.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION

  Invalid metafield value for linked option.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹NAME

  The name provided is not valid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹POSITION

  The option position provided is not valid.

* KEY\_вЂ‹MISSING\_вЂ‹IN\_вЂ‹INPUT

  A key is missing in the input.

* LINKED\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹DEFINITION\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  No valid metafield definition found for linked option.

* LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹UPDATE\_вЂ‹MISSING\_вЂ‹VALUES

  Updating the linked\_metafield of an option requires a linked\_metafield\_value for each option value.

* LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTIONS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORTED\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹SHOP

  Linked options are currently not supported for this shop.

* NO\_вЂ‹KEY\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹CREATE

  On create, this key cannot be used.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹EXISTS

  Option already exists.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Option does not exist.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹TAKEN

  An option linked to the provided metafield already exists.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹NAME\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  Option name is too long.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹EXISTS

  Option value already exists.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹CONFLICTING\_вЂ‹OPERATION

  Performing conflicting actions on an option value.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Option value does not exist.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹HAS\_вЂ‹VARIANTS

  Option value with variants linked cannot be deleted.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹NAME\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  Option value name is too long.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES\_вЂ‹OVER\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  Option values count is over the allowed limit.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹SUSPENDED

  Product is suspended.

* TOO\_вЂ‹MANY\_вЂ‹VARIANTS\_вЂ‹CREATED

  The number of option values created with the MANAGE strategy would exceed the variant limit.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹COMBINED\_вЂ‹LISTING\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹OPERATION

<a id="product-option-update-variant-strategy"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹OptionвЂ‹UpdateвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹Strategy

enum

Requires `read_products` access scope.

The set of variant strategies available for use in the `productOptionUpdate` mutation.

### Valid values

* LEAVE\_вЂ‹AS\_вЂ‹IS

  Variants are not created nor deleted in response to option values to add or delete. In cases where deleting a variant would be necessary to complete the operation, an error will be returned.

* MANAGE

  Variants are created and deleted according to the option values to add and to delete.

  If an option value is added, a new variant will be added for each existing option combination available on the product. For example, if the existing options are `Size` and `Color`, with values `S`/`XL` and `Red`/`Blue`, adding a new option value `Green` for the option `Color` will create variants with the option value combinations `S`/`Green` and `XL`/`Green`.

  If an option value is deleted, all variants referencing that option value will be deleted.

***

### Fields

* [productвЂ‹OptionвЂ‹Update.variantStrategy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productOptionUpdate#arguments-variantStrategy)

  ARGUMENT

***

### Map

#### Arguments with this enum

* [productвЂ‹OptionвЂ‹Update.variantStrategy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productOptionUpdate#arguments-variantStrategy)

enum

  An option cannot have both metafield linked and nonlinked option values.

  The metafield violates a capability restriction.

  Cannot link multiple options to the same metafield.

  Duplicated metafield value for linked option.

* DUPLICATED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹NAME

  Duplicated option name.

* DUPLICATED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Duplicated option value.

* DUPLICATED\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Duplicated value.

  Something went wrong, please try again.

  The product gift\_card attribute cannot be changed after creation.

* GIFT\_вЂ‹CARDS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ACTIVATED

  Gift card products can only be created after they have been activated.

* HANDLE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹UNIQUE

  Handle already in use. Please provide a new handle.

* ID\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED

  The id field is not allowed if identifier is provided.

* INPUT\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  The identifier value does not match the value of the corresponding field in the input.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹INPUT

  Input is not valid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹METAFIELD

  Metafield is not valid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION

  Invalid metafield value for linked option.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹PRODUCT

  Product is not valid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹VARIANT

  Product variant is not valid.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹QUANTITIES\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  Inventory quantity input exceeds the limit of 50000. Consider using separate `inventorySetQuantities` mutations.

* JOB\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Error processing request in the background job.

* LINKED\_вЂ‹METAFIELD\_вЂ‹DEFINITION\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  No valid metafield definition found for linked option.

* LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTIONS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORTED\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹SHOP

  Linked options are currently not supported for this shop.

* METAFIELD\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  The input argument `metafields` (if present) must contain the `customId` value.

* MISSING\_вЂ‹FIELD\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  The input field corresponding to the identifier is required.

* NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Resource matching the identifier was not found.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Option does not exist.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Option value does not exist.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES\_вЂ‹MISSING

  Each option must have at least one option value specified.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES\_вЂ‹OVER\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  Option values over limit.

* OPTIONS\_вЂ‹OVER\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  Options over limit.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹OPTIONS\_вЂ‹INPUT\_вЂ‹MISSING

  Must specify product options when updating variants.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹SUSPENDED

  Product is suspended.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product variant does not exist.

* VARIANTS\_вЂ‹INPUT\_вЂ‹MISSING

  Must specify variants when updating options.

* VARIANTS\_вЂ‹OVER\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  Number of product variants exceeds shop limit.

<a id="product-sort-keys"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹SortвЂ‹Keys

enum

The set of valid sort keys for the Product query.

### Valid values

* CREATED\_вЂ‹AT

  Sort by the `created_at` value.

* ID

  Sort by the `id` value.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹TOTAL

  Sort by the `inventory_total` value.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹TYPE

  Sort by the `product_type` value.

* PUBLISHED\_вЂ‹AT

  Sort by the `published_at` value.

* RELEVANCE

  Sort by relevance to the search terms when the `query` parameter is specified on the connection. Don't use this sort key when no search query is specified.

* TITLE

  Sort by the `title` value.

* UPDATED\_вЂ‹AT

  Sort by the `updated_at` value.

* VENDOR

  Sort by the `vendor` value.

***

### Fields

* [Publication.includedProducts(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Publication#field-Publication.fields.includedProducts.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  A group of [products](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product) and [collections](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Collection) that are published to an app.

  Each publication manages which products and collections display on its associated [`Channel`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Channel). Merchants can automatically publish products when they're created if [`autoPublish`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Publication#field-Publication.fields.autoPublish) is enabled, or manually control publication through publication records.

  Publications support scheduled publishing through future publish dates for online store channels, allowing merchants to coordinate product launches and promotional campaigns. The [`catalog`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Publication#field-Publication.fields.catalog) field links to pricing and availability rules specific to that publication's context.

* [Publication.products(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Publication#field-Publication.fields.products.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  A group of [products](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product) and [collections](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Collection) that are published to an app.

  Each publication manages which products and collections display on its associated [`Channel`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Channel). Merchants can automatically publish products when they're created if [`autoPublish`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Publication#field-Publication.fields.autoPublish) is enabled, or manually control publication through publication records.

  Publications support scheduled publishing through future publish dates for online store channels, allowing merchants to coordinate product launches and promotional campaigns. The [`catalog`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Publication#field-Publication.fields.catalog) field links to pricing and availability rules specific to that publication's context.

* [Shop.products(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Shop#field-Shop.fields.products.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The central configuration and settings hub for a Shopify store. Access business information, operational preferences, feature availability, and store-wide settings that control how the shop operates.

  Includes core business details like the shop name, contact emails, billing address, and currency settings. The shop configuration determines customer account requirements, available sales channels, enabled features, payment settings, and policy documents. Also provides access to shop-level resources such as staff members, fulfillment services, navigation settings, and storefront access tokens.

* [QueryвЂ‹Root.products(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/QueryRoot#field-QueryRoot.fields.products.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The schema's entry-point for queries. This acts as the public, top-level API from which all queries must start.

* [products.sortKey](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/queries/products#arguments-sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

***

<a id="product-status"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹Status

enum

The possible product statuses.

### Valid values

* ACTIVE

  The product is ready to sell and can be published to sales channels and apps. Products with an active status aren't automatically published to sales channels, such as the online store, or apps. By default, existing products are set to active.

* ARCHIVED

  The product is no longer being sold and isn't available to customers on sales channels and apps.

* DRAFT

  The product isn't ready to sell and is unavailable to customers on sales channels and apps. By default, duplicated and unarchived products are set to draft.

* UNLISTED

  The product is active but you need a direct link to view it. The product doesn't show up in search, collections, or product recommendations. It will be returned in Storefront API and Liquid only when referenced individually by handle, id, or metafield reference.This status is only visible from 2025-10 and up, is translated to active in older versions and can't be changed from unlisted in older versions.

***

### Fields

* [Product.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product#field-Product.fields.status)

  OBJECT

  The `Product` object lets you manage products in a merchantвЂ™s store.

  Products are the goods and services that merchants offer to customers. They can include various details such as title, description, price, images, and options such as size or color. You can use [product variants](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/productvariant) to create or update different versions of the same product. You can also add or update product [media](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/media). Products can be organized by grouping them into a [collection](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/collection).

  Learn more about working with [Shopify's product model](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/graphql/migrate/new-product-model/product-model-components), including limitations and considerations.

* [ProductвЂ‹CreateвЂ‹Input.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ProductCreateInput#fields-status)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields required to create a product.

* [ProductвЂ‹Input.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ProductInput#fields-status)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for creating or updating a product.

* [ProductвЂ‹SetвЂ‹Input.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ProductSetInput#fields-status)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields required to create or update a product via ProductSet mutation.

* [ProductвЂ‹UpdateвЂ‹Input.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ProductUpdateInput#fields-status)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for updating a product.

* [productвЂ‹ChangeвЂ‹Status.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productChangeStatus#arguments-status)

  ARGUMENT

* [productвЂ‹Duplicate.newStatus](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productDuplicate#arguments-newStatus)

<a id="product-variants-bulk-create-strategy"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹VariantsвЂ‹BulkвЂ‹CreateвЂ‹Strategy

enum

The set of strategies available for use on the `productVariantsBulkCreate` mutation.

### Valid values

* DEFAULT

  The default strategy. Deletes the standalone default ("Default Title") variant when it's the only variant on the product. Preserves the standalone custom variant.

* PRESERVE\_вЂ‹STANDALONE\_вЂ‹VARIANT

  Preserves the existing standalone variant when the product has only a single default ("Default Title") or a single custom variant.

* REMOVE\_вЂ‹STANDALONE\_вЂ‹VARIANT

  Deletes the existing standalone variant when the product has only a single default ("Default Title") or custom variant.

***

### Fields

* [productвЂ‹VariantsвЂ‹BulkвЂ‹Create.strategy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productVariantsBulkCreate#arguments-strategy)

  ARGUMENT

***

<a id="product-variants-bulk-create-user-error-code"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹VariantsвЂ‹BulkвЂ‹CreateвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `ProductVariantsBulkCreateUserError`.

### Valid values

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹SET\_вЂ‹NAME\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Cannot set name for an option value linked to a metafield.

* GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  Variant price must be greater than or equal to zero.

* INVALID

  Invalid input detected.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹INPUT

  Input is invalid.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹QUANTITIES\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  Inventory quantity input exceeds the limit of 50000. Consider using separate `inventorySetQuantities` mutations.

* MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹THIS\_вЂ‹PRODUCT

  Input must be for this product.

* NEED\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES

  Variant options are not enough.

* NEGATIVE\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Price cannot take a negative value.

* NO\_вЂ‹KEY\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹CREATE

  On create, this key cannot be used.

* NOT\_вЂ‹DEFINED\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹SHOP

  Input is not defined for this shop.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUES\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹NUMBER\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹UNKNOWN\_вЂ‹OPTIONS

  Variant options are more than the product options.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹SUSPENDED

  Product is suspended.

* SUBSCRIPTION\_вЂ‹VIOLATION

  You reached the limit of available SKUs in your current plan.

* TOO\_вЂ‹MANY\_вЂ‹INVENTORY\_вЂ‹LOCATIONS

  Inventory locations cannot exceed the allowed resource limit or 10.

* TRACKED\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹LOCATION\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Quantity could not be set. The location was not found.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹COMBINED\_вЂ‹LISTING\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹OPERATION

  Operation is not supported for a combined listing parent product.

* VARIANT\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹EXISTS

  Variant already exists.

* VARIANT\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹EXISTS\_вЂ‹CHANGE\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Variant options already exist. Please change the variant option(s).

***

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `ProductVariantsBulkUpdateUserError`.

* BLANK

  The input value is blank.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹SET\_вЂ‹NAME\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹LINKED\_вЂ‹OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Cannot set name for an option value linked to a metafield.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹SPECIFY\_вЂ‹BOTH

  Mutually exclusive input fields provided.

* GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  The price of the variant must be greater than or equal to zero.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹INPUT

  Input is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Metafield value is invalid.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹QUANTITIES\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  Inventory quantity input exceeds the limit of 50000. Consider using separate `inventorySetQuantities` mutations.

* MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹THIS\_вЂ‹PRODUCT

  Input must be for this product.

* MUST\_вЂ‹SPECIFY\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹PAIR

  Variant options are not enough.

* NEGATIVE\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Price cannot take a negative value.

* NO\_вЂ‹INVENTORY\_вЂ‹QUANTITES\_вЂ‹DURING\_вЂ‹UPDATE

  Inventory quantities cannot be provided during update.

* NO\_вЂ‹INVENTORY\_вЂ‹QUANTITIES\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹VARIANTS\_вЂ‹UPDATE

  Inventory quantities can only be provided during create. To update inventory for existing variants, use inventoryAdjustQuantities.

* NOT\_вЂ‹DEFINED\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹SHOP

  Input is not defined for this shop.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Option does not exist.

* OPTION\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹SUSPENDED

  Product is suspended.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product variant does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹ID\_вЂ‹MISSING

* SUBSCRIPTION\_вЂ‹VIOLATION

* TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  The input value is too long.

* TOO\_вЂ‹SHORT

<a id="product-variant-inventory-policy"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹InventoryвЂ‹Policy

enum

The valid values for the inventory policy of a product variant once it is out of stock.

### Valid values

* CONTINUE

  Customers can buy this product variant after it's out of stock.

* DENY

  Customers can't buy this product variant after it's out of stock.

***

### Fields

* [ProductвЂ‹Variant.inventoryPolicy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariant#field-ProductVariant.fields.inventoryPolicy)

  OBJECT

  The `ProductVariant` object represents a version of a [product](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product) that comes in more than one [option](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductOption), such as size or color. For example, if a merchant sells t-shirts with options for size and color, then a small, blue t-shirt would be one product variant and a large, blue t-shirt would be another.

  Use the `ProductVariant` object to manage the full lifecycle and configuration of a product's variants. Common use cases for using the `ProductVariant` object include:

  * Tracking inventory for each variant
  * Setting unique prices for each variant
  * Assigning barcodes and SKUs to connect variants to fulfillment services
  * Attaching variant-specific images and media
  * Setting delivery and tax requirements
  * Supporting product bundles, subscriptions, and selling plans

  A `ProductVariant` is associated with a parent [`Product`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product) object. `ProductVariant` serves as the central link between a product's merchandising configuration, inventory, pricing, fulfillment, and sales channels within the GraphQL Admin API schema. Each variant can reference other GraphQL types such as:

  * [`InventoryItem`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/InventoryItem): Used for inventory tracking
  * [`Image`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Image): Used for variant-specific images
  * [`SellingPlanGroup`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlanGroup): Used for subscriptions and selling plans

  Learn more about [Shopify's product model](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/graphql/migrate/new-product-model/product-model-components).

* [ProductвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹SetвЂ‹Input.inventoryPolicy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ProductVariantSetInput#fields-inventoryPolicy)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for specifying a product variant to create or update.

* [ProductвЂ‹VariantsвЂ‹BulkвЂ‹Input.inventoryPolicy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ProductVariantsBulkInput#fields-inventoryPolicy)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for specifying a product variant to create as part of a variant bulk mutation.

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

* [ProductвЂ‹Variant.inventoryPolicy](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariant#field-ProductVariant.fields.inventoryPolicy)

#### Inputs with this enum

<a id="product-variant-relationship-bulk-update-user-error-code"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹RelationshipвЂ‹BulkвЂ‹UpdateвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `ProductVariantRelationshipBulkUpdateUserError`.

### Valid values

* CHILD\_вЂ‹PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹CANNOT\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹COMBINED\_вЂ‹LISTING

  Combined listing cannot be child product variants.

* CIRCULAR\_вЂ‹REFERENCE

  A parent product variant cannot contain itself as a component.

* DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹RELATIONSHIP

  A parent product variant must not contain duplicate product variant relationships.

* EXCEEDED\_вЂ‹PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹RELATIONSHIP\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  Exceeded the maximum allowable product variant relationships in a parent product variant.

* FAILED\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹CREATE

  Unable to create parent product variant.

* FAILED\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹REMOVE

  Unable to remove product variant relationships.

* FAILED\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹UPDATE

  Unable to update product variant relationships.

* FAILED\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹UPDATE\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹PRICE

  Unable to update parent product variant price.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹QUANTITY

  Product variant relationships must have a quantity greater than 0.

* MUST\_вЂ‹SPECIFY\_вЂ‹COMPONENTS

  The product variant relationships to remove must be specified if all the parent product variant's components aren't being removed.

* NESTED\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT

  Nested parent product variants aren't supported.

* PARENT\_вЂ‹PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹CANNOT\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹COMBINED\_вЂ‹LISTING

  Combined listing cannot be parent product variants.

* PARENT\_вЂ‹PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹CANNOT\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD

  Gift cards cannot be parent product variants.

* PARENT\_вЂ‹PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹CANNOT\_вЂ‹REQUIRE\_вЂ‹SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN

  Parent product variants cannot require a selling plan.

* PARENT\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  A parent product variant ID or product ID must be provided.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹EXPANDER\_вЂ‹APP\_вЂ‹OWNERSHIP\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹EXISTS

  The products for these product variants are already owned by another App.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹RELATIONSHIP\_вЂ‹TYPE\_вЂ‹CONFLICT

  A Core type relationship cannot be added to a composite product variant with SFN type relationships.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANTS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹COMPONENTS

  Some of the provided product variants are not components of the specified parent product variant.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANTS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  The product variants were not found.

* UNEXPECTED\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Unexpected error.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹MULTIPACK\_вЂ‹RELATIONSHIP

  Multipack bundles are not supported.

* UPDATE\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  A price must be provided for a parent product variant if the price calculation is set to fixed.

***

### Fields

* [ProductвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹RelationshipвЂ‹BulkвЂ‹UpdateвЂ‹UserвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariantRelationshipBulkUpdateUserError#field-ProductVariantRelationshipBulkUpdateUserError.fields.code)

  OBJECT

  An error that occurs during the execution of `ProductVariantRelationshipBulkUpdate`.

<a id="product-variant-sort-keys"></a>

## ProductвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹SortвЂ‹Keys

enum

The set of valid sort keys for the ProductVariant query.

### Valid values

* FULL\_вЂ‹TITLE

  Sort by the `full_title` value.

* ID

  Sort by the `id` value.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹LEVELS\_вЂ‹AVAILABLE

  Sort by available inventory quantity in the location specified by the `query:"location_id:"` argument. Don't use this sort key when no `location_id` in query is specified.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹MANAGEMENT

  Sort by the `inventory_management` value.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹POLICY

  Sort by the `inventory_policy` value.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹QUANTITY

  Sort by the `inventory_quantity` value.

* NAME

  Sort by the `name` value.

* POPULAR

  Sort by the `popular` value.

* POSITION

  Sort by the `position` value.

* RELEVANCE

  Sort by relevance to the search terms when the `query` parameter is specified on the connection. Don't use this sort key when no search query is specified.

* SKU

  Sort by the `sku` value.

* TITLE

  Sort by the `title` value.

***

### Fields

* [Product.variants(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product#field-Product.fields.variants.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The `Product` object lets you manage products in a merchantвЂ™s store.

  Products are the goods and services that merchants offer to customers. They can include various details such as title, description, price, images, and options such as size or color. You can use [product variants](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/productvariant) to create or update different versions of the same product. You can also add or update product [media](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/media). Products can be organized by grouping them into a [collection](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/collection).

  Learn more about working with [Shopify's product model](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/graphql/migrate/new-product-model/product-model-components), including limitations and considerations.

* [Shop.productVariants(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Shop#field-Shop.fields.productVariants.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The central configuration and settings hub for a Shopify store. Access business information, operational preferences, feature availability, and store-wide settings that control how the shop operates.

  Includes core business details like the shop name, contact emails, billing address, and currency settings. The shop configuration determines customer account requirements, available sales channels, enabled features, payment settings, and policy documents. Also provides access to shop-level resources such as staff members, fulfillment services, navigation settings, and storefront access tokens.

* [QueryвЂ‹Root.productVariants(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/QueryRoot#field-QueryRoot.fields.productVariants.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The schema's entry-point for queries. This acts as the public, top-level API from which all queries must start.

* [productвЂ‹Variants.sortKey](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/queries/productVariants#arguments-sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

* [DeliveryвЂ‹ProfileвЂ‹Item.variants(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/DeliveryProfileItem#field-DeliveryProfileItem.fields.variants.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  Deprecated

<a id="publication-user-error-code"></a>

## PublicationвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `PublicationUserError`.

### Valid values

* BLANK

  The input value is blank.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹MODIFY\_вЂ‹APP\_вЂ‹CATALOG

  Cannot modify a catalog for an app.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹MODIFY\_вЂ‹APP\_вЂ‹CATALOG\_вЂ‹PUBLICATION

  Can't modify a publication that belongs to an app catalog.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹MODIFY\_вЂ‹MARKET\_вЂ‹CATALOG

  Cannot modify a catalog for a market.

* CANNOT\_вЂ‹MODIFY\_вЂ‹MARKET\_вЂ‹CATALOG\_вЂ‹PUBLICATION

  Can't modify a publication that belongs to a market catalog.

* CATALOG\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Catalog does not exist.

* INVALID

  The input value is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹PUBLISHABLE\_вЂ‹ID

  Publishable ID not found.

* MARKET\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Market does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹TYPE\_вЂ‹INCOMPATIBLE\_вЂ‹WITH\_вЂ‹CATALOG\_вЂ‹TYPE

  A product publication cannot be created because the catalog type associated with this publication does not permit publications of this product type.

* PUBLICATION\_вЂ‹LOCKED

  The publication is currently being modified. Please try again later.

* PUBLICATION\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Publication not found.

* PUBLICATION\_вЂ‹UPDATE\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  The limit for simultaneous publication updates has been exceeded.

* TAKEN

  The input value is already taken.

* TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  The input value is too long.

* TOO\_вЂ‹SHORT

  The input value is too short.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹PUBLICATION\_вЂ‹ACTION

  Can't perform this action on a publication.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹PUBLISHABLE\_вЂ‹TYPE

  A catalog publication can only contain products.

***

## QuantityвЂ‹PriceвЂ‹BreakвЂ‹SortвЂ‹Keys

enum

The set of valid sort keys for the QuantityPriceBreak query.

### Valid values

* ID

  Sort by the `id` value.

* MINIMUM\_вЂ‹QUANTITY

  Sort by the `minimum_quantity` value.

***

### Fields

* [PriceвЂ‹ListвЂ‹Price.quantityPriceBreaks(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceListPrice#field-PriceListPrice.fields.quantityPriceBreaks.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  Pricing for a [`ProductVariant`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariant) on a [`PriceList`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceList). Represents the variant's price, compare-at price, and whether the price is fixed or calculated using percentage-based adjustments. The [`PriceListPriceOriginType`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/PriceListPriceOriginType) distinguishes between prices set directly on the price list (fixed) and prices calculated using the price list's adjustment configuration (relative).

  Learn more about [building catalogs with different pricing models](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/markets/build-catalog).

* [ProductвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹ContextualвЂ‹Pricing.quantityPriceBreaks(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariantContextualPricing#field-ProductVariantContextualPricing.fields.quantityPriceBreaks.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The price of a product variant in a specific country. Prices vary between countries.

***

* [ProductвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹ContextualвЂ‹Pricing.quantityPriceBreaks(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariantContextualPricing#field-ProductVariantContextualPricing.fields.quantityPriceBreaks.arguments.sortKey)

<a id="quantity-pricing-by-variant-user-error-code"></a>

## QuantityвЂ‹PricingвЂ‹ByвЂ‹VariantвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `QuantityPricingByVariantUserError`.

### Valid values

* BLANK

  The input value is blank.

* GENERIC\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Something went wrong when trying to update quantity pricing. Please try again later.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  Price list and fixed price currency mismatch.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹INPUT\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹VARIANT

  Prices to add inputs must be unique by variant id.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹LOCAL\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD\_вЂ‹ISSUANCE\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  The issuance currency of a local currency gift card must match the price list currency.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹LOCAL\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  The price of a local currency gift card cannot exceed the maximum gift card purchase limit.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Fixed price's variant not found.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹DELETE\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FIXED

  Price is not fixed.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹DELETE\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Fixed price's variant not found.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Price List does not exist.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹CURRENCY\_вЂ‹MISMATCH

  Price list and quantity price break currency mismatch.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹INPUT\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹MIN

  Quantity price breaks to add inputs must be unique by variant id and minimum quantity.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹FAILED\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹SAVE

  Failed to save quantity price break.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹INVALID

  Invalid quantity price break.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  Exceeded the allowed number of quantity price breaks per variant.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MIN\_вЂ‹HIGHER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULES\_вЂ‹MAX

  Quantity price break miniumum is higher than the quantity rule maximum.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MIN\_вЂ‹LOWER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULES\_вЂ‹MIN

  Quantity price break miniumum is less than the quantity rule minimum.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MIN\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹MULTIPLE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULES\_вЂ‹INCREMENT

  Quantity price break miniumum is not multiple of the quantity rule increment.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Quantity price break's fixed price not found.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Quantity price break variant not found.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹DELETE\_вЂ‹BY\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹ID\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Variant to delete by is not found.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹DELETE\_вЂ‹FAILED

  Failed to delete quantity price break.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹DELETE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Quantity price break not found.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹CATALOG\_вЂ‹CONTEXT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORTED

  Quantity rule catalog context not supported.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹INPUT\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹VARIANT

  Quantity rules to add inputs must be unique by variant id.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹INCREMENT\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹MINIMUM

  Quantity rule increment is greater than minimum.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹INCREMENT\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹LESS\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹ONE

  Quantity rule increment is less than one.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹INCREMENT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹MULTIPLE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹MIN

  Quantity rule increment must be a multiple of the quantity price break minimum.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MAX\_вЂ‹LOWER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹MIN

  Quantity rule maximum is less than the quantity price break minimum.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MAXIMUM\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹LESS\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹ONE

  Quantity rule maximum is less than one.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MAXIMUM\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹MULTIPLE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹INCREMENT

  Quantity rule maximum is not a multiple of increment.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MIN\_вЂ‹HIGHER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹MIN

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MINIMUM\_вЂ‹GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹MAXIMUM

  Quantity rule minimum is greater than maximum.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MINIMUM\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹LESS\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹ONE

  Quantity rule minimum is less than one.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹MINIMUM\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹MULTIPLE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹INCREMENT

  Quantity rule minimum is not a multiple of increment.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹ADD\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Quantity rule variant not found.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹DELETE\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Quantity rule not found.

* QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹DELETE\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

<a id="quantity-rule-origin-type"></a>

## QuantityвЂ‹RuleвЂ‹OriginвЂ‹Type

enum

The origin of quantity rule on a price list.

### Valid values

* FIXED

  Quantity rule is explicitly defined.

* RELATIVE

  Quantity rule falls back to the relative rule.

***

### Fields

* [PriceвЂ‹List.quantityRules(originType)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceList#field-PriceList.fields.quantityRules.arguments.originType)

  ARGUMENT

  A list that defines pricing for [product variants](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariant). Price lists override default product prices with either fixed prices or percentage-based adjustments.

  Each price list associates with a [`Catalog`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/Catalog) to determine which customers see the pricing. The catalog's context rules control when the price list applies, such as for specific markets, company locations, or apps.

  Learn how to [support different pricing models](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/markets/build-catalog).

* [QuantityвЂ‹Rule.originType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/QuantityRule#field-QuantityRule.fields.originType)

  OBJECT

  The quantity rule for the product variant in a given context.

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

* [QuantityвЂ‹Rule.originType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/QuantityRule#field-QuantityRule.fields.originType)

#### Arguments with this enum

* [PriceвЂ‹List.quantityRules(originType)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/PriceList#field-PriceList.fields.quantityRules.arguments.originType)

---
<a id="quantity-rule-user-error-code"></a>

## QuantityвЂ‹RuleвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `QuantityRuleUserError`.

### Valid values

* BLANK

  The input value is blank.

* CATALOG\_вЂ‹CONTEXT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORT\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULES

  Quantity rules can be associated only with company location catalogs or catalogs associated with compatible markets.

* DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹INPUT\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹VARIANT

  Quantity rule inputs must be unique by variant id.

* GENERIC\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Something went wrong when trying to save the quantity rule. Please try again later.

* GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  Value must be greater than or equal to 1.

* INCREMENT\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹MINIMUM

  Increment must be lower than or equal to the minimum.

* INCREMENT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹MULTIPLE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹MINIMUM

  Increment must be a multiple of all quantity price break minimums associated with this variant in the specified price list.

* MAXIMUM\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹LOWER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹MINIMUM

  Maximum must be greater than or equal to all quantity price break minimums associated with this variant in the specified price list.

* MAXIMUM\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹MULTIPLE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹INCREMENT

  The maximum must be a multiple of the increment.

* MINIMUM\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹MAXIMUM

  Minimum must be lower than or equal to the maximum.

* MINIMUM\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹HIGHER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹BREAK\_вЂ‹MINIMUM

  Minimum must be less than or equal to all quantity price break minimums associated with this variant in the specified price list.

* MINIMUM\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹MULTIPLE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹INCREMENT

  The minimum must be a multiple of the increment.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Price list does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product variant ID does not exist.

* VARIANT\_вЂ‹QUANTITY\_вЂ‹RULE\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Quantity rule for variant associated with the price list provided does not exist.

***

enum

---
<a id="refund-line-item-restock-type"></a>

## RefundвЂ‹LineвЂ‹ItemвЂ‹RestockвЂ‹Type

enum

The type of restock performed for a particular refund line item.

### Valid values

* CANCEL

  The refund line item was canceled. Use this when restocking unfulfilled line items.

* LEGACY\_вЂ‹RESTOCK

  Deprecated. The refund line item was restocked, without specifically beingidentified as a return or cancelation. This value is not accepted when creating new refunds.

* NO\_вЂ‹RESTOCK

  Refund line item was not restocked.

* RETURN

  The refund line item was returned. Use this when restocking line items that were fulfilled.

***

### Fields

* [RefundвЂ‹LineвЂ‹Item.restockType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/RefundLineItem#field-RefundLineItem.fields.restockType)

  OBJECT

  A [`Product`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product) or [`ProductVariant`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariant) being refunded from an order. Each refund line item tracks the quantity, pricing, and restocking details for items returned to the merchant.

  The refund line item links to the original [`LineItem`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/LineItem) from the [`Order`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order) and includes financial information such as the refunded price, subtotal, and taxes in both shop and presentment currencies. The [`restockType`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/RefundLineItem#field-RefundLineItem.fields.restockType) field indicates whether and how the merchant restocks the returned items to inventory, while the [`location`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/RefundLineItem#field-RefundLineItem.fields.location) field specifies where restocking occurs.

* [RefundвЂ‹LineвЂ‹ItemвЂ‹Input.restockType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/RefundLineItemInput#fields-restockType)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields required to reimburse line items on a refund.

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

* [RefundвЂ‹LineвЂ‹Item.restockType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/RefundLineItem#field-RefundLineItem.fields.restockType)

#### Inputs with this enum

* [RefundвЂ‹LineвЂ‹ItemвЂ‹Input.restockType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/RefundLineItemInput#fields-restockType)

---
<a id="refund-method-allocation"></a>

## RefundвЂ‹MethodвЂ‹Allocation

enum

The different methods that a refund amount can be allocated to.

### Valid values

* ORIGINAL\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHODS

  The refund is to original payment methods.

* STORE\_вЂ‹CREDIT

  The refund is to store credit.

***

### Fields

* [Order.suggestedRefund(refundMethodAllocation)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order#field-Order.fields.suggestedRefund.arguments.refundMethodAllocation)

  ARGUMENT

  The `Order` object represents a customer's request to purchase one or more products from a store. Use the `Order` object to handle the complete purchase lifecycle from checkout to fulfillment.

  Use the `Order` object when you need to:

  * Display order details on customer account pages or admin dashboards.
  * Create orders for phone sales, wholesale customers, or subscription services.
  * Update order information like shipping addresses, notes, or fulfillment status.
  * Process returns, exchanges, and partial refunds.
  * Generate invoices, receipts, and shipping labels.

  The `Order` object serves as the central hub connecting customer information, product details, payment processing, and fulfillment data within the GraphQL Admin API schema.

  ***

  **Note:** Only the last 60 days\&#39; worth of orders from a store are accessible from the \<code>Order\</code> object by default. If you want to access older records, then you need to \<a href="https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/access-scopes#orders-permissions">request access to all orders\</a>. If your app is granted access, then you can add the \<code>\<span class="PreventFireFoxApplyingGapToWBR">read\<wbr/>\_all\<wbr/>\_orders\</span>\</code>, \<code>\<span class="PreventFireFoxApplyingGapToWBR">read\<wbr/>\_orders\</span>\</code>, and \<code>\<span class="PreventFireFoxApplyingGapToWBR">write\<wbr/>\_orders\</span>\</code> scopes.

  ***

  ***

  **Caution:** Only use orders data if it\&#39;s required for your app\&#39;s functionality. Shopify will restrict \<a href="https://shopify.dev/docs/api/usage/access-scopes#requesting-specific-permissions">access to scopes\</a> for apps that don\&#39;t have a legitimate use for the associated data.

  ***

  Learn more about [building apps for orders and fulfillment](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment).

* [Return.suggestedFinancialOutcome(refundMethodAllocation)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Return#field-Return.fields.suggestedFinancialOutcome.arguments.refundMethodAllocation)

  ARGUMENT

  The `Return` object represents the intent of a buyer to ship one or more items from an order back to a merchant or a third-party fulfillment location. A return is associated with an [order](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order) and can include multiple return [line items](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/LineItem). Each return has a [status](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps#return-statuses), which indicates the state of the return.

  Use the `Return` object to capture the financial, logistical, and business intent of a return. For example, you can identify eligible items for a return and issue customers a refund for returned items on behalf of the merchant.

  Learn more about providing a [return management workflow](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps/build-return-management) for merchants. You can also manage [exchanges](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps/manage-exchanges), [reverse fulfillment orders](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps/manage-reverse-fulfillment-orders), and [reverse deliveries](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps/manage-reverse-deliveries) on behalf of merchants.

***

### Map

#### Arguments with this enum

* [Order.suggestedRefund(refundMethodAllocation)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order#field-Order.fields.suggestedRefund.arguments.refundMethodAllocation)
* [Return.suggestedFinancialOutcome(refundMethodAllocation)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Return#field-Return.fields.suggestedFinancialOutcome.arguments.refundMethodAllocation)

## ResourceвЂ‹AlertвЂ‹Severity

enum

The possible severity levels for a resource alert.

### Valid values

* CRITICAL

  Indicates a critical alert. For example, a blocked app.

* DEFAULT

  Indicates a neutral alert. For example, an accepted dispute.

* INFO

  Indicates an informative alert. For example, an escalated dispute.

* SUCCESS

  Indicates a success alert. For example, a winning a dispute.

* WARNING

  Indicates an informative alert. For example, a new dispute.

* ERROR

  Deprecated

<a id="return-error-code"></a>

## ReturnвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `ReturnUserError`.

### Valid values

* ALREADY\_вЂ‹EXISTS

  The requested resource already exists.

* BLANK

  The input value is blank.

* CREATION\_вЂ‹FAILED

  A requested resource could not be created.

* EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  The input value should be equal to the value allowed.

* FEATURE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ENABLED

  A required feature is not enabled.

* GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN

  The input value should be greater than the minimum allowed value.

* GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  The input value should be greater than or equal to the minimum value allowed.

* INCLUSION

  The input value isn't included in the list.

* INTERNAL\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Unexpected internal error happened.

* INVALID

  The input value is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹STATE

  A resource was not in the correct state for the operation to succeed.

* LESS\_вЂ‹THAN

  The input value should be less than the maximum value allowed.

* LESS\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  The input value should be less than or equal to the maximum value allowed.

* MISSING\_вЂ‹PERMISSION

  The user does not have permission to perform the operation.

* NOT\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹NUMBER

  The input value is not a number.

* NOT\_вЂ‹EDITABLE

  A requested item is not editable.

* NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  A requested item could not be found.

* NOTIFICATION\_вЂ‹FAILED

  A requested notification could not be sent.

* PRESENT

  The input value needs to be blank.

* TAKEN

  The input value is already taken.

* TOO\_вЂ‹BIG

  The input value is too big.

* TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  The input value is too long.

* TOO\_вЂ‹MANY\_вЂ‹ARGUMENTS

  Too many arguments provided.

* TOO\_вЂ‹SHORT

  The input value is too short.

* WRONG\_вЂ‹LENGTH

  The input value is the wrong length.

***

### Fields

* [ReturnвЂ‹UserвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ReturnUserError#field-ReturnUserError.fields.code)

  OBJECT

  An error that occurs during the execution of a return mutation.

***

  The `Return` object represents the intent of a buyer to ship one or more items from an order back to a merchant or a third-party fulfillment location. A return is associated with an [order](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order) and can include multiple return [line items](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/LineItem). Each return has a [status](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps#return-statuses), which indicates the state of the return.

A return is associated with an [order](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order) and can include multiple return [line items](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/LineItem). Each return has a [status](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps#return-statuses), which indicates the state of the return.

  Use the `Return` object to capture the financial, logistical, and business intent of a return. For example, you can identify eligible items for a return and issue customers a refund for returned items on behalf of the merchant.

  Learn more about providing a [return management workflow](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps/build-return-management) for merchants. You can also manage [exchanges](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps/manage-exchanges), [reverse fulfillment orders](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps/manage-reverse-fulfillment-orders), and [reverse deliveries](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/returns-apps/manage-reverse-deliveries) on behalf of merchants.
