<a id="return-reason"></a>

## ReturnвЂ‹Reason

enum

The reason for returning the return line item.

### Valid values

* COLOR

  The item is returned because the buyer did not like the color. Displays as **Color**.

* DEFECTIVE

  The item is returned because it is damaged or defective. Displays as **Damaged or defective**.

* NOT\_вЂ‹AS\_вЂ‹DESCRIBED

  The item is returned because it was not as described. Displays as **Item not as described**.

* OTHER

  The item is returned for another reason. For this value, a return reason note is also provided. Displays as **Other**.

* SIZE\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LARGE

  The item is returned because the size was too large. Displays as **Size was too large**.

* SIZE\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹SMALL

  The item is returned because the size was too small. Displays as **Size was too small**.

* STYLE

  The item is returned because the buyer did not like the style. Displays as **Style**.

* UNKNOWN

  The item is returned because of an unknown reason. Displays as **Unknown**.

* UNWANTED

  The item is returned because the customer changed their mind. Displays as **Customer changed their mind**.

* WRONG\_вЂ‹ITEM

  The item is returned because the customer received the wrong one. Displays as **Received the wrong item**.

***

### Fields

* [ReturnвЂ‹LineвЂ‹ItemвЂ‹Input.returnReason](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ReturnLineItemInput#fields-returnReason)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for a return line item.

* [ReturnвЂ‹RequestвЂ‹LineвЂ‹ItemвЂ‹Input.returnReason](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ReturnRequestLineItemInput#fields-returnReason)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for a return line item.

<a id="sale-action-type"></a>

## SaleвЂ‹ActionвЂ‹Type

enum

The possible order action types for a sale.

### Valid values

* ORDER

  A purchase or charge.

* RETURN

  A removal or return.

* UNKNOWN

  An unknown order action. Represents new actions that may be added in future versions.

* UPDATE

  A change to the price, taxes, or discounts for a prior purchase.

***

### Fields

* [AdditionalвЂ‹FeeвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/AdditionalFeeSale#field-AdditionalFeeSale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with an additional fee charge.

* [AdjustmentвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/AdjustmentSale#field-AdjustmentSale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with an order price adjustment.

* [DutyвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/DutySale#field-DutySale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a duty charge.

* [FeeвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/FeeSale#field-FeeSale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a fee.

* [GiftвЂ‹CardвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/GiftCardSale#field-GiftCardSale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a gift card.

* [ProductвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductSale#field-ProductSale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a product.

* [Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/Sale#fields-actionType)

  INTERFACE

  An individual sale record associated with a sales agreement. Every money value in an order's sales data is represented in the currency's smallest unit. When amounts are divided across multiple line items, such as taxes or order discounts, the amounts might not divide evenly across all of the line items on the order. To address this, the remaining currency units that couldn't be divided evenly are allocated one at a time, starting with the first line item, until they are all accounted for. In aggregate, the values sum up correctly. In isolation, one line item might have a different tax or discount amount than another line item of the same price, before taxes and discounts. This is because the amount could not be divided evenly across the items. The allocation of currency units across line items is immutable. After they are allocated, currency units are never reallocated or redistributed among the line items.

* [ShippingвЂ‹LineвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShippingLineSale#field-ShippingLineSale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a shipping charge.

* [TipвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/TipSale#field-TipSale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a tip.

* [UnknownвЂ‹Sale.actionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/UnknownSale#field-UnknownSale.fields.actionType)

  OBJECT

  This is represents new sale types that have been added in future API versions. You may update to a more recent API version to receive additional details about this sale.

***

<a id="sale-line-type"></a>

## SaleвЂ‹LineвЂ‹Type

enum

The possible line types for a sale record. One of the possible order line types for a sale is an adjustment. Sales adjustments occur when a refund is issued for a line item that is either more or less than the total value of the line item. Examples are restocking fees and goodwill payments. When this happens, Shopify produces a sales agreement with sale records for each line item that is returned or refunded and an additional sale record for the adjustment (for example, a restocking fee). The sales records for the returned or refunded items represent the reversal of the original line item sale value. The additional adjustment sale record represents the difference between the original total value of all line items that were refunded, and the actual amount refunded.

### Valid values

* ADDITIONAL\_вЂ‹FEE

  An additional fee.

* ADJUSTMENT

  A sale adjustment.

* DUTY

  A duty charge.

* FEE

  A fee charge.

* GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD

  A gift card.

* PRODUCT

  A product purchased, returned or exchanged.

* SHIPPING

  A shipping cost.

* TIP

  A tip added by the customer.

* UNKNOWN

  An unknown sale line. Represents new types that may be added in future versions.

***

### Fields

* [AdditionalвЂ‹FeeвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/AdditionalFeeSale#field-AdditionalFeeSale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with an additional fee charge.

* [AdjustmentвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/AdjustmentSale#field-AdjustmentSale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with an order price adjustment.

* [DutyвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/DutySale#field-DutySale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a duty charge.

* [FeeвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/FeeSale#field-FeeSale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a fee.

* [GiftвЂ‹CardвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/GiftCardSale#field-GiftCardSale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a gift card.

* [ProductвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductSale#field-ProductSale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a product.

* [Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/Sale#fields-lineType)

  INTERFACE

  An individual sale record associated with a sales agreement. Every money value in an order's sales data is represented in the currency's smallest unit. When amounts are divided across multiple line items, such as taxes or order discounts, the amounts might not divide evenly across all of the line items on the order. To address this, the remaining currency units that couldn't be divided evenly are allocated one at a time, starting with the first line item, until they are all accounted for. In aggregate, the values sum up correctly. In isolation, one line item might have a different tax or discount amount than another line item of the same price, before taxes and discounts. This is because the amount could not be divided evenly across the items. The allocation of currency units across line items is immutable. After they are allocated, currency units are never reallocated or redistributed among the line items.

* [ShippingвЂ‹LineвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShippingLineSale#field-ShippingLineSale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a shipping charge.

* [TipвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/TipSale#field-TipSale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  A sale associated with a tip.

* [UnknownвЂ‹Sale.lineType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/UnknownSale#field-UnknownSale.fields.lineType)

  OBJECT

  This is represents new sale types that have been added in future API versions. You may update to a more recent API version to receive additional details about this sale.

  ARGUMENT

  The quantities of an inventory item at a specific location. Each inventory level connects one [`InventoryItem`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/InventoryItem) to one [`Location`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Location), tracking multiple quantity states like available, on-hand, incoming, and committed.

  The [`quantities`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/InventoryLevel#field-InventoryLevel.fields.quantities) field provides access to different inventory states. Learn [more about inventory states and relationships](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/orders-fulfillment/inventory-management-apps/manage-quantities-states#inventory-object-relationships).

<a id="script-tag-display-scope"></a>

## ScriptвЂ‹TagвЂ‹DisplayвЂ‹Scope

enum

The page or pages on the online store where the script should be included.

### Valid values

* ONLINE\_вЂ‹STORE

  Include the script only on the web storefront.

#### Deprecated valid values

* ALL

  Deprecated

* ORDER\_вЂ‹STATUS

  Deprecated

***

### Fields

* [ScriptвЂ‹Tag.displayScope](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ScriptTag#field-ScriptTag.fields.displayScope)

  OBJECT

  **Theme app extensions:**

  If your app integrates with a Shopify theme and you plan to submit it to the Shopify App Store, you must use theme app extensions instead of Script tags. Script tags can only be used with vintage themes. [Learn more](https://shopify.dev/apps/online-store#what-integration-method-should-i-use).

  **Script tag deprecation:**

  Script tags will be sunset for the **Order status** page on August 28, 2025. [Upgrade to Checkout Extensibility](https://www.shopify.com/plus/upgrading-to-checkout-extensibility) before this date. [Shopify Scripts](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/liquid/objects#script) will continue to work alongside Checkout Extensibility until August 28, 2025.

  A script tag represents remote JavaScript code that is loaded into the pages of a shop's storefront or the **Order status** page of checkout.

* [ScriptвЂ‹TagвЂ‹Input.displayScope](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/ScriptTagInput#fields-displayScope)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for a script tag. This input object is used when creating or updating a script tag to specify its URL, where it should be included, and how it will be cached.

***

## SearchвЂ‹ResultвЂ‹Type

enum

Specifies the type of resources to be returned from a search.

### Valid values

* ARTICLE

  An article.

* BALANCE\_вЂ‹TRANSACTION

  A balance transaction.

* BLOG

  A blog.

* COLLECTION

* CUSTOMER

* DISCOUNT\_вЂ‹REDEEM\_вЂ‹CODE

  A code discount redeem code.

* DRAFT\_вЂ‹ORDER

* FILE

  A file.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹TRANSFER

  An inventory transfer.

* ORDER

* PAGE

  A page.

* PRICE\_вЂ‹RULE

* PRODUCT

* URL\_вЂ‹REDIRECT

  A URL redirect.

***

### Fields

* [SavedвЂ‹Search.resourceType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SavedSearch#field-SavedSearch.fields.resourceType)

  OBJECT

  A representation of a search query in the Shopify admin used on resource index views. Preserves complex queries with search terms and filters, enabling merchants to quickly access frequently used data views. For example, a saved search can be applied to the product index table to filter products. The query string combines free-text search terms with structured filters to narrow results based on resource attributes.

  The search applies to a specific resource type such as [`Customer`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Customer), [`Product`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product), [`Order`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order), or [`Collection`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Collection) objects.

* [SavedвЂ‹SearchвЂ‹CreateвЂ‹Input.resourceType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SavedSearchCreateInput#fields-resourceType)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields to create a saved search.

* [Shop.search(types)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Shop#field-Shop.fields.search.arguments.types)

  ARGUMENT

  The central configuration and settings hub for a Shopify store. Access business information, operational preferences, feature availability, and store-wide settings that control how the shop operates.

  Includes core business details like the shop name, contact emails, billing address, and currency settings. The shop configuration determines customer account requirements, available sales channels, enabled features, payment settings, and policy documents. Also provides access to shop-level resources such as staff members, fulfillment services, navigation settings, and storefront access tokens.

***

  Which day of the month, between 1-31.

* WEEKDAY

  Which day of the week, between 1-7.

* YEARDAY

  Which days of the month and year, month between 1-12, and day between 1-31.

***

### Fields

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹Anchor.type](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlanAnchor#field-SellingPlanAnchor.fields.type)

  OBJECT

  Specifies the date when delivery or fulfillment is completed by a merchant for a given time cycle. You can also define a cutoff for which customers are eligible to enter this cycle and the desired behavior for customers who start their subscription inside the cutoff period.

  Some example scenarios where anchors can be useful to implement advanced delivery behavior:

  * A merchant starts fulfillment on a specific date every month.
  * A merchant wants to bill the 1st of every quarter.
  * A customer expects their delivery every Tuesday.

  For more details, see [About Selling Plans](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/selling-plans#anchors).

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹AnchorвЂ‹Input.type](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SellingPlanAnchorInput#fields-type)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields required to create or update a selling plan anchor.

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

#### Inputs with this enum

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹AnchorвЂ‹Input.type](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SellingPlanAnchorInput#fields-type)

<a id="selling-plan-category"></a>

## SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹Category

enum

The category of the selling plan. For the `OTHER` category, you must fill out our [request form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeU18Xmw0Q61V8wdH-dfGafFqIBfRchQKUO8WAF3yJTvgyyZQ/viewform), where we'll review your request for a new purchase option.

### Valid values

* OTHER

  The selling plan is for anything not in one of the other categories.

* PRE\_вЂ‹ORDER

  The selling plan is for pre-orders.

* SUBSCRIPTION

  The selling plan is for subscriptions.

* TRY\_вЂ‹BEFORE\_вЂ‹YOU\_вЂ‹BUY

  The selling plan is for try before you buy purchases.

***

### Fields

* [SellingвЂ‹Plan.category](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlan#field-SellingPlan.fields.category)

  OBJECT

  How a product can be sold and purchased through recurring billing or deferred purchase options. Defines the specific terms for subscriptions, pre-orders, or try-before-you-buy offers, including when to bill customers, when to fulfill orders, and what pricing adjustments to apply.

  Each selling plan has billing, delivery, and pricing policies that control the purchase experience. The plan's [`options`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlan#field-SellingPlan.fields.options) and [`category`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlan#field-SellingPlan.fields.category) help merchants organize and report on different selling strategies. Plans are grouped within a [`SellingPlanGroup`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlanGroup) that associates them with [`Product`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Product) and [`ProductVariant`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ProductVariant) objects.

  ***

  **Caution:** Selling plans and associated records are automatically deleted 48 hours after a merchant uninstalls the \<a href="https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/App">\<code>App\</code>\</a> that created them. Back up these records if you need to restore them later.

  ***

  Learn more about [selling plans](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/selling-plans/build-a-selling-plan).

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹Input.category](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SellingPlanInput#fields-category)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields to create or update a selling plan.

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

#### Inputs with this enum

<a id="selling-plan-fixed-delivery-policy-pre-anchor-behavior"></a>

## SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹FixedвЂ‹DeliveryвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹PreвЂ‹AnchorвЂ‹Behavior

enum

The fulfillment or delivery behavior of the first fulfillment when the orderis placed before the anchor.

### Valid values

* ASAP

  Orders placed can be fulfilled / delivered immediately. Orders placed inside a cutoff can be fulfilled / delivered at the next anchor.

* NEXT

  Orders placed can be fulfilled / delivered at the next anchor date. Orders placed inside a cutoff will skip the next anchor and can be fulfilled / delivered at the following anchor.

***

  OBJECT

  Represents a fixed selling plan delivery policy.

<a id="selling-plan-fulfillment-trigger"></a>

## SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹FulfillmentвЂ‹Trigger

enum

Describes what triggers fulfillment.

### Valid values

* ANCHOR

  Use the anchor values to calculate fulfillment date.

* ASAP

  As soon as possible.

* EXACT\_вЂ‹TIME

  At an exact time defined by the fulfillment\_exact\_time field.

* UNKNOWN

  Unknown. Usually to be determined in the future.

<a id="selling-plan-group-user-error-code"></a>

## SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹GroupвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `SellingPlanGroupUserError`.

### Valid values

* BILLING\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹DELIVERY\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹TYPES\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹THE\_вЂ‹SAME

  Billing and delivery policy types must be the same.

* BILLING\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹INTERVAL\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LARGE

  Billing policy's interval is too large.

* BLANK

  The input value is blank.

* CHECKOUT\_вЂ‹CHARGE\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹TYPE\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹MATCH

  A fixed billing policy's checkout charge value and type must match.

* DELIVERY\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹INTERVAL\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LARGE

  Delivery policy's interval is too large.

* EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  The input value should be equal to the value allowed.

* ERROR\_вЂ‹ADDING\_вЂ‹RESOURCE\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹GROUP

  Could not add the resource to the selling plan group.

* FULFILLMENT\_вЂ‹EXACT\_вЂ‹TIME\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED

  A fixed billing policy's fulfillment\_exact\_time must not be present when the fulfillment\_trigger isn't EXACT\_TIME.

* FULFILLMENT\_вЂ‹EXACT\_вЂ‹TIME\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  A fixed billing policy's fulfillment\_exact\_time can't be blank when the fulfillment\_trigger is EXACT\_TIME.

* GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN

  The input value should be greater than the minimum allowed value.

* GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  The input value should be greater than or equal to the minimum value allowed.

* GROUP\_вЂ‹COULD\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹DELETED

  Selling plan group could not be deleted.

* GROUP\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Selling plan group does not exist.

* INCLUSION

  The input value isn't included in the list.

* INVALID

  The input value is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹INPUT

  The input submitted is invalid.

* LESS\_вЂ‹THAN

  The input value should be less than the maximum value allowed.

* LESS\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹EQUAL\_вЂ‹TO

  The input value should be less than or equal to the maximum value allowed.

* NOT\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹NUMBER

  The input value is not a number.

* NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  The record with the ID used as the input value couldn't be found.

* ONLY\_вЂ‹NEED\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹BILLING\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹TYPE

  Only one billing policy type can be defined.

* ONLY\_вЂ‹NEED\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹CHECKOUT\_вЂ‹CHARGE\_вЂ‹VALUE

  A fixed billing policy's checkout charge can have at most one value.

* ONLY\_вЂ‹NEED\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹DELIVERY\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹TYPE

  Only one delivery policy type can be defined.

* ONLY\_вЂ‹NEED\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹PRICING\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹TYPE

  Only one pricing policy type can be defined.

* ONLY\_вЂ‹NEED\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹PRICING\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹VALUE

  Only one pricing policy adjustment value type can be defined.

* ONLY\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹FIXED\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹RECURRING\_вЂ‹BILLING

  A selling plan can't have both fixed and recurring billing policies.

* ONLY\_вЂ‹ONE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹FIXED\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹RECURRING\_вЂ‹DELIVERY

  A selling plan can't have both fixed and recurring delivery policies.

* PLAN\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Selling plan does not exist.

* PLAN\_вЂ‹ID\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹SPECIFIED\_вЂ‹TO\_вЂ‹UPDATE

  Selling plan ID must be specified to update.

* PRESENT

  The input value needs to be blank.

* PRICING\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹VALUE\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹TYPE\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹MATCH

  Pricing policy's adjustment value and adjustment type must match.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product does not exist.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹VARIANT\_вЂ‹DOES\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹EXIST

  Product variant does not exist.

* REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE\_вЂ‹CHARGE\_вЂ‹EXACT\_вЂ‹TIME\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED

  A fixed billing policy's remaining\_balance\_charge\_exact\_time must not be present when the remaining\_balance\_charge\_trigger isn't EXACT\_TIME.

* REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE\_вЂ‹CHARGE\_вЂ‹EXACT\_вЂ‹TIME\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  A fixed billing policy's remaining\_balance\_charge\_exact\_time can't be blank when the remaining\_balance\_charge\_trigger is EXACT\_TIME.

* REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE\_вЂ‹CHARGE\_вЂ‹TIME\_вЂ‹AFTER\_вЂ‹CHECKOUT\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹ZERO

  A fixed billing policy's remaining\_balance\_charge\_time\_after\_checkout must be present and greater than zero when the remaining\_balance\_charge\_trigger is TIME\_AFTER\_CHECKOUT.

* REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE\_вЂ‹CHARGE\_вЂ‹TRIGGER\_вЂ‹NO\_вЂ‹REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹PARTIAL\_вЂ‹PERCENTAGE\_вЂ‹CHECKOUT\_вЂ‹CHARGE

  A fixed billing policy's remaining\_balance\_charge\_trigger can't be NO\_REMAINING\_BALANCE when the checkout\_charge\_type is PERCENTAGE and checkout\_charge\_value is less than 100.

* REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE\_вЂ‹CHARGE\_вЂ‹TRIGGER\_вЂ‹NO\_вЂ‹REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹PRICE\_вЂ‹CHECKOUT\_вЂ‹CHARGE

  A fixed billing policy's remaining\_balance\_charge\_trigger can't be NO\_REMAINING\_BALANCE when the checkout\_charge\_type is PRICE.

* REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE\_вЂ‹CHARGE\_вЂ‹TRIGGER\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹FULL\_вЂ‹CHECKOUT

  A fixed billing policy's remaining\_balance\_charge\_trigger must be NO\_REMAINING\_BALANCE when the checkout\_charge\_type is PERCENTAGE and checkout\_charge\_value is 100.

* RESOURCE\_вЂ‹LIST\_вЂ‹CONTAINS\_вЂ‹INVALID\_вЂ‹IDS

  The selling plan list provided contains 1 or more invalid IDs.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹ANCHORS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED

  A fixed delivery policy's anchors must not be present when the fulfillment\_trigger isn't ANCHOR.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹ANCHORS\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  A fixed delivery policy's anchors must be present when the fulfillment\_trigger is ANCHOR.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹BILLING\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹DELIVERY\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹ANCHORS\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹EQUAL

  Selling plan's billing and delivery policies anchors must be equal.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹BILLING\_вЂ‹CYCLE\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹MULTIPLE\_вЂ‹OF\_вЂ‹DELIVERY\_вЂ‹CYCLE

  Selling plan's billing cycle must be a multiple of delivery cycle.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹BILLING\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹MISSING

  Missing billing policy.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹COUNT\_вЂ‹LOWER\_вЂ‹BOUND

  Must include at least one selling plan.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹COUNT\_вЂ‹UPPER\_вЂ‹BOUND

  Exceeded the selling plan limit (31).

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹DELIVERY\_вЂ‹POLICY\_вЂ‹MISSING

  Missing delivery policy.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹NAME

  Cannot have multiple selling plans with the same name.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹DUPLICATE\_вЂ‹OPTIONS

  Cannot have multiple selling plans with the same options.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹FIXED\_вЂ‹PRICING\_вЂ‹POLICIES\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  A fixed selling plan can have at most one pricing policy.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹MAX\_вЂ‹CYCLES\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹GREATER\_вЂ‹THAN\_вЂ‹MIN\_вЂ‹CYCLES

  Selling plan's billing policy max cycles must be greater than min cycles.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹MISSING\_вЂ‹OPTION2\_вЂ‹LABEL\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹GROUP

  Cannot define option2 on this selling plan as there's no label on the parent selling plan group.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹MISSING\_вЂ‹OPTION3\_вЂ‹LABEL\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹GROUP

  Cannot define option3 on this selling plan as there's no label on the parent selling plan group.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹OPTION2\_вЂ‹REQUIRED\_вЂ‹AS\_вЂ‹DEFINED\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹GROUP

  Selling plan's option2 is required because option2 exists.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹OPTION3\_вЂ‹REQUIRED\_вЂ‹AS\_вЂ‹DEFINED\_вЂ‹ON\_вЂ‹PARENT\_вЂ‹GROUP

  Selling plan's option3 is required because option3 exists.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹PRICING\_вЂ‹POLICIES\_вЂ‹LIMIT

  Selling plans can't have more than 2 pricing policies.

* SELLING\_вЂ‹PLAN\_вЂ‹PRICING\_вЂ‹POLICIES\_вЂ‹MUST\_вЂ‹CONTAIN\_вЂ‹A\_вЂ‹FIXED\_вЂ‹PRICING\_вЂ‹POLICY

  Selling plan's pricing policies must contain one fixed pricing policy.

* TAKEN

  The input value is already taken.

* TOO\_вЂ‹BIG

  The input value is too big.

* TOO\_вЂ‹LONG

  The input value is too long.

* TOO\_вЂ‹SHORT

  The input value is too short.

* WRONG\_вЂ‹LENGTH

  The input value is the wrong length.

***

  OBJECT

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹RecurringвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹Input.interval](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SellingPlanRecurringBillingPolicyInput#fields-interval)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields required to create or update a recurring billing policy.

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹RecurringвЂ‹DeliveryвЂ‹Policy.interval](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlanRecurringDeliveryPolicy#field-SellingPlanRecurringDeliveryPolicy.fields.interval)

<a id="selling-plan-pricing-policy-adjustment-type"></a>

## SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹PricingвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹AdjustmentвЂ‹Type

enum

Represents a selling plan pricing policy adjustment type.

### Valid values

* FIXED\_вЂ‹AMOUNT

  Fixed amount off adjustment.

* PERCENTAGE

  Percentage off adjustment.

* PRICE

  Price of the policy.

***

### Fields

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹FixedвЂ‹PricingвЂ‹Policy.adjustmentType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlanFixedPricingPolicy#field-SellingPlanFixedPricingPolicy.fields.adjustmentType)

  OBJECT

  Represents the pricing policy of a subscription or deferred purchase option selling plan. The selling plan fixed pricing policy works with the billing and delivery policy to determine the final price. Discounts are divided among fulfillments. For example, a subscription with a $10 discount and two deliveries will have a $5 discount applied to each delivery.

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹FixedвЂ‹PricingвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹Input.adjustmentType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SellingPlanFixedPricingPolicyInput#fields-adjustmentType)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields required to create or update a fixed selling plan pricing policy.

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹PricingвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹Base.adjustmentType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/SellingPlanPricingPolicyBase#fields-adjustmentType)

  INTERFACE

  Represents selling plan pricing policy common fields.

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹RecurringвЂ‹PricingвЂ‹Policy.adjustmentType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlanRecurringPricingPolicy#field-SellingPlanRecurringPricingPolicy.fields.adjustmentType)

  OBJECT

  Represents a recurring selling plan pricing policy. It applies after the fixed pricing policy. By using the afterCycle parameter, you can specify the cycle when the recurring pricing policy comes into effect. Recurring pricing policies are not available for deferred purchase options.

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹RecurringвЂ‹PricingвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹Input.adjustmentType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SellingPlanRecurringPricingPolicyInput#fields-adjustmentType)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields required to create or update a recurring selling plan pricing policy.

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹CycleвЂ‹PriceвЂ‹Adjustment.adjustmentType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionCyclePriceAdjustment#field-SubscriptionCyclePriceAdjustment.fields.adjustmentType)

  OBJECT

  Represents a Subscription Line Pricing Cycle Adjustment.

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹PricingвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹CycleвЂ‹DiscountsвЂ‹Input.adjustmentType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SubscriptionPricingPolicyCycleDiscountsInput#fields-adjustmentType)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for an array containing all pricing changes for each billing cycle.

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

<a id="selling-plan-recurring-delivery-policy-pre-anchor-behavior"></a>

## SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹RecurringвЂ‹DeliveryвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹PreвЂ‹AnchorвЂ‹Behavior

enum

The fulfillment or delivery behaviors of the first fulfillment when the orderis placed before the anchor.

### Valid values

* ASAP

  The orders placed can be fulfilled or delivered immediately. The orders placed inside a cutoff can be fulfilled or delivered at the next anchor.

* NEXT

  The orders placed can be fulfilled or delivered at the next anchor date. The orders placed inside a cutoff will skip the next anchor and can be fulfilled or delivered at the following anchor.

***

### Fields

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹RecurringвЂ‹DeliveryвЂ‹Policy.preAnchorBehavior](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlanRecurringDeliveryPolicy#field-SellingPlanRecurringDeliveryPolicy.fields.preAnchorBehavior)

<a id="selling-plan-remaining-balance-charge-trigger"></a>

## SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹RemainingвЂ‹BalanceвЂ‹ChargeвЂ‹Trigger

enum

When to capture the payment for the remaining amount due.

### Valid values

* EXACT\_вЂ‹TIME

  At an exact time defined by the remaining\_balance\_charge\_exact\_time field.

* NO\_вЂ‹REMAINING\_вЂ‹BALANCE

  When there's no remaining balance to be charged after checkout.

* ON\_вЂ‹FULFILLMENT

  When the order is fulfilled.

* TIME\_вЂ‹AFTER\_вЂ‹CHECKOUT

  After the duration defined by the remaining\_balance\_charge\_time\_after\_checkout field.

***

### Fields

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹FixedвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹Policy.remainingBalanceChargeTrigger](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SellingPlanFixedBillingPolicy#field-SellingPlanFixedBillingPolicy.fields.remainingBalanceChargeTrigger)

  OBJECT

  The fixed selling plan billing policy defines how much of the price of the product will be billed to customer at checkout. If there is an outstanding balance, it determines when it will be paid.

* [SellingвЂ‹PlanвЂ‹FixedвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹PolicyвЂ‹Input.remainingBalanceChargeTrigger](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/SellingPlanFixedBillingPolicyInput#fields-remainingBalanceChargeTrigger)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields required to create or update a fixed billing policy.

***

<a id="server-pixel-status"></a>

## ServerвЂ‹PixelвЂ‹Status

enum

The current state of a server pixel.

### Valid values

* CONNECTED

  This server pixel is connected: it will stream customer events to the endpoint if it is configured properly.

* DISCONNECTED\_вЂ‹CONFIGURED

  This server pixel is disconnected: it does not stream events to the endpoint and an endpoint address has been added to the server pixel.

* DISCONNECTED\_вЂ‹UNCONFIGURED

  This server pixel is disconnected and unconfigured: it does not stream events to the endpoint and no endpoint address had been added to the server pixel.

***

<a id="shopify-payments-balance-transaction-payout-status"></a>

## ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹BalanceвЂ‹TransactionвЂ‹PayoutвЂ‹Status

enum

The payout status of the balance transaction.

### Valid values

* ACTION\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  The transaction requires action before it can be paid out.

* CANCELED

  The payout has been canceled by Shopify.

* FAILED

  The payout has been declined by the bank.

* PAID

  The payout has been successfully deposited into the bank.

* PENDING

  The transaction has not been assigned a payout yet.

* SCHEDULED

  The payout has been created and had transactions assigned to it, but it has not yet been submitted to the bank.

* IN\_вЂ‹TRANSIT

  Deprecated

***

### Fields

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹BalanceвЂ‹TransactionвЂ‹AssociatedвЂ‹Payout.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransactionAssociatedPayout#field-ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransactionAssociatedPayout.fields.status)

<a id="shopify-payments-business-type"></a>

## ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹BusinessвЂ‹Type

enum

The business type of a Shopify Payments account.

### Valid values

* CORPORATION

  The business type is a corporation.

* FREE\_вЂ‹ZONE\_вЂ‹ESTABLISHMENT

  The business type is a free zone establishment.

* FREE\_вЂ‹ZONE\_вЂ‹LLC

  The business type is a free zone LLC.

* GOVERNMENT

  The business type is a government.

* INCORPORATED\_вЂ‹PARTNERSHIP

  The business type is an incorporated partnership.

* INDIVIDUAL

  The business is an individual.

* LLC

  The business type is a Limited Liability Company.

* NON\_вЂ‹PROFIT

  The business type is a non profit.

* NON\_вЂ‹PROFIT\_вЂ‹INCORPORATED

  The business type is a non profit (incorporated).

* NON\_вЂ‹PROFIT\_вЂ‹REGISTERED\_вЂ‹CHARITY

  The business type is a non profit (registered charity).

* NON\_вЂ‹PROFIT\_вЂ‹UNINCORPORATED

  The business type is a non profit (unincorporated).

* NON\_вЂ‹PROFIT\_вЂ‹UNINCORPORATED\_вЂ‹ASSOCIATION

  The business type is a non profit (unincorporated\_association).

* NOT\_вЂ‹SET

  The business type is not set. This is usually because onboarding is incomplete.

* PARTNERSHIP

  The business type is a partnership.

* PRIVATE\_вЂ‹CORPORATION

  The business type is a private corporation.

* PRIVATE\_вЂ‹MULTI\_вЂ‹MEMBER\_вЂ‹LLC

  The business type is a private multi member LLC.

* PRIVATE\_вЂ‹PARTNERSHIP

  The business type is a private partnership.

* PRIVATE\_вЂ‹SINGLE\_вЂ‹MEMBER\_вЂ‹LLC

  The business type is a private single member LLC.

* PRIVATE\_вЂ‹UNINCORPORATED\_вЂ‹ASSOCIATION

  The business type is a private unincorporated association.

* PUBLIC\_вЂ‹COMPANY

  The business type is a public company.

* PUBLIC\_вЂ‹CORPORATION

  The business type is a public corporation.

* PUBLIC\_вЂ‹PARTNERSHIP

  The business type is a public partnership.

* SOLE\_вЂ‹ESTABLISHMENT

  The business type is a sole establishment.

* SOLE\_вЂ‹PROP

  The business type is a sole proprietorship.

* UNINCORPORATED\_вЂ‹PARTNERSHIP

  The business type is an unincorporated partnership.

***

### Fields

enum

The reason for the dispute provided by the cardholder's bank.

### Valid values

* BANK\_вЂ‹CANNOT\_вЂ‹PROCESS

  The customer's bank can't process the charge.

* CREDIT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹PROCESSED

  The customer claims that the purchased product was returned or the transaction was otherwise canceled, but you haven't yet provided a refund or credit.

* CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹INITIATED

  The customer initiated the dispute. Contact the customer for additional details on why the payment was disputed.

* DEBIT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹AUTHORIZED

  The customer's bank can't proceed with the debit since it hasn't been authorized.

* DUPLICATE

  The customer claims they were charged multiple times for the same product or service.

* FRAUDULENT

  The cardholder claims that they didnвЂ™t authorize the payment.

* GENERAL

  The dispute is uncategorized, so you should contact the customer for additional details to find out why the payment was disputed.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹ACCOUNT\_вЂ‹DETAILS

  The customer account associated with the purchase is incorrect.

* INSUFFICIENT\_вЂ‹FUNDS

  The customer's bank account has insufficient funds.

* NONCOMPLIANT

  The card issuer believes the disputed transaction doesn't conform to the network rules. These disputes occur when transactions don't meet card network requirements and may incur additional network fees if escalated for resolution.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹RECEIVED

  The customer claims they did not receive the products or services purchased.

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹UNACCEPTABLE

  The product or service was received but was defective, damaged, or not as described.

* SUBSCRIPTION\_вЂ‹CANCELLED

  The customer claims that you continued to charge them after a subscription was canceled.

* UNRECOGNIZED

  The customer doesnвЂ™t recognize the payment appearing on their card statement.

***

  OBJECT

<a id="shopify-payments-payout-status"></a>

## ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹PayoutвЂ‹Status

enum

The transfer status of the payout.

### Valid values

* CANCELED

  The payout has been canceled by Shopify.

* FAILED

  The payout has been declined by the bank.

* PAID

  The payout has been successfully deposited into the bank.

* SCHEDULED

  The payout has been created and had transactions assigned to it, but it has not yet been submitted to the bank.

* IN\_вЂ‹TRANSIT

  Deprecated

***

### Fields

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹Payout.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout#field-ShopifyPaymentsPayout.fields.status)

  OBJECT

  A transfer of funds between a merchant's Shopify Payments balance and their [`ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount). Provides the [net amount](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout#field-ShopifyPaymentsPayout.fields.net), [issue date](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout#field-ShopifyPaymentsPayout.fields.issuedAt), and current [`ShopifyPaymentsPayoutStatus`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/ShopifyPaymentsPayoutStatus).

  The payout includes a [`ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSummary`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSummary) that breaks down fees and gross amounts by transaction type, such as charges, refunds, and adjustments. The [`ShopifyPaymentsPayoutTransactionType`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/ShopifyPaymentsPayoutTransactionType) indicates whether funds move into the bank account (deposit) or back to Shopify Payments (withdrawal).

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹Payout.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout#field-ShopifyPaymentsPayout.fields.status)

---
<a id="shopify-payments-payout-transaction-type"></a>

## ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹PayoutвЂ‹TransactionвЂ‹Type

enum

The possible transaction types for a payout.

### Valid values

* DEPOSIT

  The payout is a deposit.

* WITHDRAWAL

  The payout is a withdrawal.

***

### Fields

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹Account.payouts(transactionType)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsAccount#field-ShopifyPaymentsAccount.fields.payouts.arguments.transactionType)

  ARGUMENT

  Financial account information for merchants using Shopify Payments. Tracks current balances across all supported currencies, payout schedules, and [`ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransaction`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransaction) records.

  The account includes configuration details such as [`ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount) objects for receiving [`ShopifyPaymentsPayout`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout) transfers, statement descriptors that appear on customer credit card statements, and the [`ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSchedule`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSchedule) that determines when funds transfer to your bank. Access balance transactions to review individual charges, refunds, and adjustments that affect your account balance. Query payouts to track money movement between your Shopify Payments balance and bank accounts.

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹BankвЂ‹Account.payouts(transactionType)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount#field-ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount.fields.payouts.arguments.transactionType)

  ARGUMENT

  A bank account that can receive payouts.

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹Payout.transactionType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout#field-ShopifyPaymentsPayout.fields.transactionType)

  OBJECT

  A transfer of funds between a merchant's Shopify Payments balance and their [`ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBankAccount). Provides the [net amount](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout#field-ShopifyPaymentsPayout.fields.net), [issue date](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout#field-ShopifyPaymentsPayout.fields.issuedAt), and current [`ShopifyPaymentsPayoutStatus`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/ShopifyPaymentsPayoutStatus).

  The payout includes a [`ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSummary`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayoutSummary) that breaks down fees and gross amounts by transaction type, such as charges, refunds, and adjustments. The [`ShopifyPaymentsPayoutTransactionType`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/enums/ShopifyPaymentsPayoutTransactionType) indicates whether funds move into the bank account (deposit) or back to Shopify Payments (withdrawal).

***

## ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹SourceвЂ‹Type

enum

The possible source types for a balance transaction.

### Valid values

* ADJUSTMENT

  The adjustment source type.

* ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹REVERSAL

  The adjustment\_reversal source type.

* CHARGE

  The charge source type.

* DISPUTE

  The dispute source type.

* REFUND

  The refund source type.

* SYSTEM\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT

  The system\_adjustment source type.

* TRANSFER

  The transfer source type.

***

### Fields

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹BalanceвЂ‹Transaction.sourceType](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransaction#field-ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransaction.fields.sourceType)

  OBJECT

  A transaction that contributes to a Shopify Payments account balance. Records money movement from charges, refunds, payouts, adjustments, or other payment activities. Includes the gross amount, processing fees, and resulting net amount that affects the account balance. Links to the source of the transaction and associated [`ShopifyPaymentsPayout`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout) details, with optional references to [`Order`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order) objects or adjustment reasons when applicable.

  The shopify\_source\_debit\_reversal transaction type.

* STRIPE\_вЂ‹FEE

  The stripe\_fee transaction type.

* TAX\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹CREDIT

  The tax\_adjustment\_credit transaction type.

* TAX\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹CREDIT\_вЂ‹REVERSAL

  The tax\_adjustment\_credit\_reversal transaction type.

* TAX\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹DEBIT

  The tax\_adjustment\_debit transaction type.

* TAX\_вЂ‹ADJUSTMENT\_вЂ‹DEBIT\_вЂ‹REVERSAL

  The tax\_adjustment\_debit\_reversal transaction type.

* TRANSFER

  The transfer transaction type.

* TRANSFER\_вЂ‹CANCEL

  The transfer\_cancel transaction type.

* TRANSFER\_вЂ‹FAILURE

  The transfer\_failure transaction type.

* TRANSFER\_вЂ‹REFUND

  The transfer\_refund transaction type.

* VAT\_вЂ‹REFUND\_вЂ‹CREDIT

  The vat\_refund\_credit transaction type.

* VAT\_вЂ‹REFUND\_вЂ‹CREDIT\_вЂ‹REVERSAL

  The vat\_refund\_credit\_reversal transaction type.

***

* [ShopifyвЂ‹PaymentsвЂ‹BalanceвЂ‹Transaction.type](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransaction#field-ShopifyPaymentsBalanceTransaction.fields.type)

  OBJECT

  A transaction that contributes to a Shopify Payments account balance. Records money movement from charges, refunds, payouts, adjustments, or other payment activities. Includes the gross amount, processing fees, and resulting net amount that affects the account balance. Links to the source of the transaction and associated [`ShopifyPaymentsPayout`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyPaymentsPayout) details, with optional references to [`Order`](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/Order) objects or adjustment reasons when applicable.

***

<a id="shopify-protect-eligibility-status"></a>

## ShopifyвЂ‹ProtectвЂ‹EligibilityвЂ‹Status

enum

The status of an order's eligibility for protection against fraudulent chargebacks by Shopify Protect.

### Valid values

* ELIGIBLE

  The order is eligible for protection against fraudulent chargebacks. If an order is updated, the order's eligibility may change and protection could be removed.

* NOT\_вЂ‹ELIGIBLE

  The order isn't eligible for protection against fraudulent chargebacks.

* PENDING

  The eligibility of the order is pending and has not yet been determined.

***

### Fields

  OBJECT

  The eligibility details of an order's protection against fraudulent chargebacks by Shopify Protect.

***

---
<a id="shopify-protect-status"></a>

## ShopifyвЂ‹ProtectвЂ‹Status

enum

The status of an order's protection with Shopify Protect.

### Valid values

* ACTIVE

  The protection for the order is active and eligible for reimbursement against fraudulent chargebacks. If an order is updated, the order's eligibility may change and protection could become inactive.

* INACTIVE

  The protection for an order isn't active because the order didn't meet eligibility requirements.

* NOT\_вЂ‹PROTECTED

  The order received a chargeback but the order wasn't protected because it didn't meet coverage requirements.

* PENDING

  The protection for the order is pending and has not yet been determined.

* PROTECTED

  The order received a fraudulent chargeback and it was protected.

***

### Fields

* [ShopifyвЂ‹ProtectвЂ‹OrderвЂ‹Summary.status](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopifyProtectOrderSummary#field-ShopifyProtectOrderSummary.fields.status)

  OBJECT

  A summary of Shopify Protect details for an order.

***

  OBJECT

  Includes core business details like the shop name, contact emails, billing address, and currency settings. <a id="shop-pay-payment-request-receipt-processing-status-error-code"></a>

## ShopвЂ‹PayвЂ‹PaymentвЂ‹RequestвЂ‹ReceiptвЂ‹ProcessingвЂ‹StatusвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

A standardized error code, independent of the payment provider.

### Valid values

* AMOUNT\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹SMALL

  The amount is too small.

* CALL\_вЂ‹ISSUER

  Call the card issuer.

* CARD\_вЂ‹DECLINED

  The card was declined.

* CONFIG\_вЂ‹ERROR

  There is an error in the gateway or merchant configuration.

* CVV\_вЂ‹ATTEMPTS\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  Too many failed CVV verification attempts.

* EXPIRED\_вЂ‹CARD

  The card is expired.

* FRAUD\_вЂ‹SUSPECTED

  The card issuer has flagged the transaction as potentially fraudulent.

* GENERIC\_вЂ‹ERROR

  There was an unknown error with processing the payment.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹ADDRESS

  The address does not match the card number.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹CVC

  The CVC does not match the card number.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹NUMBER

  The card number is incorrect.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹PIN

  The entered PIN is incorrect.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹ZIP

  The ZIP or postal code does not match the card number.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹AMOUNT

  The amount is either too high or too low for the provider.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹COUNTRY

  The payment method is not available in the customer's country.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹CVC

  The format of the CVC is incorrect.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹EXPIRY\_вЂ‹DATE

  The format of the expiry date is incorrect.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹NUMBER

  The format of the card number is incorrect.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹UNAVAILABLE

  The payment method is momentarily unavailable.

* PICK\_вЂ‹UP\_вЂ‹CARD

  The card has been reported as lost or stolen, and the card issuer has requested that the merchant keep the card and call the number on the back.

* PROCESSING\_вЂ‹ERROR

  There was an error while processing the payment.

* TEST\_вЂ‹MODE\_вЂ‹LIVE\_вЂ‹CARD

  A real card was used but the gateway was in test mode.

* THREE\_вЂ‹D\_вЂ‹SECURE\_вЂ‹FAILED

  The 3D Secure check failed.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹FEATURE

  The gateway or merchant configuration doesn't support a feature, such as network tokenization.

***

### Fields

* [ShopвЂ‹PayвЂ‹PaymentвЂ‹RequestвЂ‹ReceiptвЂ‹ProcessingвЂ‹Status.errorCode](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/ShopPayPaymentRequestReceiptProcessingStatus#field-ShopPayPaymentRequestReceiptProcessingStatus.fields.errorCode)

  OBJECT

  The processing status of a Shop Pay payment request. Represents the different states a payment request can be in during its lifecycle, from initial creation through to completion or failure.

***

### Map

<a id="staff-member-permission"></a>

## StaffвЂ‹MemberвЂ‹Permission

enum

Represents access permissions for a staff member.

### Valid values

* APPLICATIONS

  The staff member can manage and install apps and channels.

* CHANNELS

  The staff member can manage and install sales channels.

* CREATE\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹EDIT\_вЂ‹CUSTOMERS

  The staff member can create and edit customers.

* CREATE\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹EDIT\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARDS

  The staff member can create and edit gift cards.

* CREATE\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹EDIT\_вЂ‹MARKETS

  The staff member can create and edit markets.

* CUSTOMERS

  The staff member can view customers.

* DASHBOARD

  The staff member can view the Shopify Home page, which includes sales information and other shop data.

* DEACTIVATE\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARDS

  The staff member can deactivate gift cards.

* DELETE\_вЂ‹CUSTOMERS

  The staff member can delete customers.

* DELETE\_вЂ‹MARKETS

  The staff member can delete markets.

* DOMAINS

  The staff member can view, buy, and manage domains.

* DRAFT\_вЂ‹ORDERS

  The staff member can create, update, and delete draft orders.

* EDIT\_вЂ‹ORDERS

  The staff member can update orders.

* ERASE\_вЂ‹CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹DATA

  The staff member can erase customer private data.

* EXPORT\_вЂ‹CUSTOMERS

  The staff member can export customers.

* EXPORT\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARDS

  The staff member can export gift cards.

* GIFT\_вЂ‹CARDS

  The staff member can view, create, issue, and export gift cards to a CSV file.

* LINKS

  The staff member can view and modify links and navigation menus.

* LOCATIONS

  The staff member can create, update, and delete locations where inventory is stocked or managed.

* MARKETING

  The staff member can view and create discount codes and automatic discounts, and export discounts to a CSV file.

* MARKETING\_вЂ‹SECTION

  The staff member can view, create, and automate marketing campaigns.

* MERGE\_вЂ‹CUSTOMERS

  The staff member can merge customers.

* ORDERS

  The staff member can view, create, update, delete, and cancel orders, and receive order notifications. The staff member can still create draft orders without this permission.

* OVERVIEWS

  The staff member can view the Overview and Live view pages, which include sales information, and other shop and sales channels data.

* PAGES

  The staff member can view, create, update, publish, and delete blog posts and pages.

* PAY\_вЂ‹ORDERS\_вЂ‹BY\_вЂ‹VAULTED\_вЂ‹CARD

  The staff member can pay for an order by using a vaulted card.

* PREFERENCES

  The staff member can view the preferences and configuration of a shop.

* PRODUCTS

  The staff member can view, create, import, and update products, collections, and inventory.

* REPORTS

  The staff member can view and create all reports, which includes sales information and other shop data.

* REQUEST\_вЂ‹CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹DATA

  The staff member can request customer private data.

* THEMES

  The staff member can view, update, and publish themes.

* VIEW\_вЂ‹MARKETS

  The staff member can view markets.

#### Deprecated valid values

* FULL

  Deprecated

* TRANSLATIONS

  Deprecated

***

### Fields

* [StaffвЂ‹MemberвЂ‹PrivateвЂ‹Data.permissions](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StaffMemberPrivateData#field-StaffMemberPrivateData.fields.permissions)

  Deprecated

  INPUT OBJECT

  The required fields and parameters to generate the URL upload an" asset to Shopify.

  Deprecated in favor of [StagedUploadInput](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StagedUploadInput), which is used by the [stagedUploadsCreate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/stagedUploadsCreate).

***

<a id="staged-upload-target-generate-upload-resource"></a>

## StagedвЂ‹UploadвЂ‹TargetвЂ‹GenerateвЂ‹UploadвЂ‹Resource

enum

The resource type to receive.

### Valid values

* BULK\_вЂ‹MUTATION\_вЂ‹VARIABLES

  Represents bulk mutation variables.

  For example, bulk mutation variables can be used for bulk operations using the [bulkOperationRunMutation mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/bulkOperationRunMutation).

* COLLECTION\_вЂ‹IMAGE

  An image associated with a collection.

  For example, after uploading an image, you can use the [collectionUpdate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/collectionUpdate) to add the image to a collection.

* DISPUTE\_вЂ‹FILE\_вЂ‹UPLOAD

  Represents a file associated with a dispute.

  For example, after uploading the file, you can add the file to a dispute using the [disputeEvidenceUpdate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/disputeEvidenceUpdate).

* FILE

  Represents any file other than HTML.

  For example, after uploading the file, you can add the file to the [Files page](https://shopify.com/admin/settings/files) in Shopify admin using the [fileCreate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/fileCreate).

* IMAGE

  An image.

  For example, after uploading an image, you can add the image to a product using the [productCreateMedia mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productCreateMedia) or to the [Files page](https://shopify.com/admin/settings/files) in Shopify admin using the [fileCreate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/fileCreate).

* MODEL\_вЂ‹3D

  A Shopify hosted 3d model.

  For example, after uploading the 3d model, you can add the 3d model to a product using the [productCreateMedia mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productCreateMedia).

* RETURN\_вЂ‹LABEL

  Represents a label associated with a return.

  For example, once uploaded, this resource can be used to [create a ReverseDelivery](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/unstable/mutations/reverseDeliveryCreateWithShipping).

* SHOP\_вЂ‹IMAGE

  An image.

  For example, after uploading the image, you can add the image to the [Files page](https://shopify.com/admin/settings/files) in Shopify admin using the [fileCreate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/fileCreate).

* URL\_вЂ‹REDIRECT\_вЂ‹IMPORT

  Represents a redirect CSV file.

  Example usage: This resource can be used for creating a [UrlRedirectImport](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/2022-04/objects/UrlRedirectImport) object for use in the [urlRedirectImportCreate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/urlRedirectImportCreate).

* VIDEO

  A Shopify-hosted video.

  For example, after uploading the video, you can add the video to a product using the [productCreateMedia mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/productCreateMedia) or to the [Files page](https://shopify.com/admin/settings/files) in Shopify admin using the [fileCreate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/fileCreate).

* PRODUCT\_вЂ‹IMAGE

  Deprecated

***

### Fields

* [StageвЂ‹ImageвЂ‹Input.resource](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/StageImageInput#fields-resource)

  INPUT OBJECT

  An image to be uploaded.

  Deprecated in favor of [StagedUploadInput](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StagedUploadInput), which is used by the [stagedUploadsCreate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/stagedUploadsCreate).

* [StagedвЂ‹UploadвЂ‹Input.resource](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/StagedUploadInput#fields-resource)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The input fields for generating staged upload targets.

* [StagedвЂ‹UploadвЂ‹TargetвЂ‹GenerateвЂ‹Input.resource](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/input-objects/StagedUploadTargetGenerateInput#fields-resource)

  INPUT OBJECT

  The required fields and parameters to generate the URL upload an" asset to Shopify.

  Deprecated in favor of [StagedUploadInput](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StagedUploadInput), which is used by the [stagedUploadsCreate mutation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/stagedUploadsCreate).

***

<a id="standard-metafield-definition-enable-user-error-code"></a>

## StandardвЂ‹MetafieldвЂ‹DefinitionвЂ‹EnableвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `StandardMetafieldDefinitionEnableUserError`.

### Valid values

* ADMIN\_вЂ‹ACCESS\_вЂ‹INPUT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED

  Admin access can only be specified for app-owned metafield definitions.

* CAPABILITY\_вЂ‹CANNOT\_вЂ‹BE\_вЂ‹DISABLED

  The metafield definition capability cannot be disabled.

* INVALID

  The input value is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹CAPABILITY

  The metafield definition capability is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹INPUT\_вЂ‹COMBINATION

  The input combination is invalid.

* LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  The maximum number of definitions per owner type has been exceeded.

* OWNER\_вЂ‹TYPE\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹USE\_вЂ‹AS\_вЂ‹ADMIN\_вЂ‹FILTERS

  You have reached the maximum allowed definitions to be used as admin filters.

* TAKEN

  The input value is already taken.

* TEMPLATE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  The standard metafield definition template was not found.

* TYPE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED\_вЂ‹FOR\_вЂ‹CONDITIONS

  The definition type is not eligible to be used as collection condition.

* UNSTRUCTURED\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹EXISTS

  The namespace and key is already in use for a set of your metafields.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹PINNING

  The metafield definition does not support pinning.

***

### Fields

* [StandardвЂ‹MetafieldвЂ‹DefinitionвЂ‹EnableвЂ‹UserвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StandardMetafieldDefinitionEnableUserError#field-StandardMetafieldDefinitionEnableUserError.fields.code)

  OBJECT

  An error that occurs during the execution of `StandardMetafieldDefinitionEnable`.

<a id="store-credit-account-credit-user-error-code"></a>

## StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹UserвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Possible error codes that can be returned by `StoreCreditAccountCreditUserError`.

### Valid values

* ACCOUNT\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  The store credit account could not be found.

* CREDIT\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  The operation would cause the account's credit limit to be exceeded.

* EXPIRES\_вЂ‹AT\_вЂ‹IN\_вЂ‹PAST

  The expiry date must be in the future.

* MISMATCHING\_вЂ‹CURRENCY

  The currency provided does not match the currency of the store credit account.

* NEGATIVE\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹ZERO\_вЂ‹AMOUNT

  A positive amount must be used to credit a store credit account.

* OWNER\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Owner does not exist.

* UNSUPPORTED\_вЂ‹CURRENCY

  The currency provided is not currently supported.

***

### Fields

* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹UserвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountCreditUserError#field-StoreCreditAccountCreditUserError.fields.code)

  OBJECT

  An error that occurs during the execution of `StoreCreditAccountCredit`.

Possible error codes that can be returned by `StoreCreditAccountDebitUserError`.

* INSUFFICIENT\_вЂ‹FUNDS

* MISMATCHING\_вЂ‹CURRENCY

  The currency provided does not match the currency of the store credit account.

* NEGATIVE\_вЂ‹OR\_вЂ‹ZERO\_вЂ‹AMOUNT

<a id="store-credit-system-event"></a>

## StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹SystemвЂ‹Event

enum

The event that caused the store credit account transaction.

### Valid values

* ADJUSTMENT

  An adjustment was made to the store credit account.

* ORDER\_вЂ‹CANCELLATION

  Store credit was returned when an authorized payment was voided.

* ORDER\_вЂ‹PAYMENT

  Store credit was used as payment for an order.

* ORDER\_вЂ‹REFUND

  Store credit was refunded from an order.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹FAILURE

  A store credit payment was reverted due to another payment method failing.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹RETURNED

  A smaller amount of store credit was captured than was originally authorized.

* TAX\_вЂ‹FINALIZATION

  Tax finalization affected the store credit payment.

***

### Fields

* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountCreditTransaction#field-StoreCreditAccountCreditTransaction.fields.event)

  OBJECT

  A credit transaction which increases the store credit account balance.

* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹DebitвЂ‹RevertвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountDebitRevertTransaction#field-StoreCreditAccountDebitRevertTransaction.fields.event)

  OBJECT

  A debit revert transaction which increases the store credit account balance. Debit revert transactions are created automatically when a [store credit account debit transaction](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountDebitTransaction) is reverted.

  Store credit account debit transactions are reverted when an order is cancelled, refunded or in the event of a payment failure at checkout. The amount added to the balance is equal to the amount reverted on the original credit.

* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹DebitвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountDebitTransaction#field-StoreCreditAccountDebitTransaction.fields.event)

  OBJECT

  A debit transaction which decreases the store credit account balance.

* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹ExpirationвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountExpirationTransaction#field-StoreCreditAccountExpirationTransaction.fields.event)

  OBJECT

  An expiration transaction which decreases the store credit account balance. Expiration transactions are created automatically when a [store credit account credit transaction](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountCreditTransaction) expires.

  The amount subtracted from the balance is equal to the remaining amount of the credit transaction.

* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/StoreCreditAccountTransaction#fields-event)

  INTERFACE

  Interface for a store credit account transaction.

***

### Map

#### Fields with this enum

* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountCreditTransaction#field-StoreCreditAccountCreditTransaction.fields.event)
* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹DebitвЂ‹RevertвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountDebitRevertTransaction#field-StoreCreditAccountDebitRevertTransaction.fields.event)
* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹DebitвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountDebitTransaction#field-StoreCreditAccountDebitTransaction.fields.event)
* [StoreвЂ‹CreditвЂ‹AccountвЂ‹ExpirationвЂ‹Transaction.event](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/StoreCreditAccountExpirationTransaction#field-StoreCreditAccountExpirationTransaction.fields.event)

<a id="subscription-billing-attempt-error-code"></a>

## SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

The possible error codes associated with making billing attempts. The error codes supplement the `error_message` to provide consistent results and help with dunning management.

### Valid values

* AMOUNT\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LARGE

  The amount exceeds the limit for this payment method.

* AMOUNT\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹SMALL

  The amount is too small.

* AUTHENTICATION\_вЂ‹ERROR

  There was an error during the payment authentication.

* AUTHENTICATION\_вЂ‹FAILED

  Authentication failed during payment processing.

* AUTHENTICATION\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  Additional authentication is required to complete the payment.

* BUYER\_вЂ‹CANCELED\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD

  Payment method was canceled by buyer.

* CALL\_вЂ‹ISSUER

  The card issuer requires the cardholder to call them.

* CANCELLED\_вЂ‹PAYMENT

  The payment was cancelled.

* CARD\_вЂ‹DECLINED

  The card was declined by the payment processor.

* CARD\_вЂ‹NUMBER\_вЂ‹INCORRECT

  Card number was incorrect.

* CONFIRMATION\_вЂ‹REJECTED

  The payment confirmation was rejected.

* CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹INVALID

  Customer is invalid.

* CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Customer was not found.

* DO\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹HONOR

  The card issuer declined the transaction with a 'do not honor' response.

* EXPIRED\_вЂ‹BUYER\_вЂ‹ACTION

  The required buyer action has expired.

* EXPIRED\_вЂ‹CARD

  The card has expired.

* EXPIRED\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD

  Payment method is expired.

* FRAUD\_вЂ‹SUSPECTED

  Fraud was suspected.

* FREE\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED

  Gift cards must have a price greater than zero.

* GENERIC\_вЂ‹ERROR

  A generic payment error occurred.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹ADDRESS

  The billing address provided is incorrect.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹NUMBER

  The card number entered is incorrect.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹ZIP

  The postal/ZIP code provided is incorrect.

* INSUFFICIENT\_вЂ‹FUNDS

  Insufficient funds.

* INSUFFICIENT\_вЂ‹INVENTORY

  Not enough inventory found.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹BILLING\_вЂ‹ADDRESS

  The billing address is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹CURRENCY

  The currency is not supported for this payment method.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹BILLING\_вЂ‹AGREEMENT

  The billing agreement ID or the transaction ID for the customer's payment method is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹EXPIRY\_вЂ‹DATE

  The expiry date of the card provided is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹NUMBER

  The card number is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD

  Payment method is invalid. Please update or create a new payment method.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹PURCHASE\_вЂ‹TYPE

  The purchase type is invalid for this payment method.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹SHIPPING\_вЂ‹ADDRESS

  The shipping address is either missing or invalid.

* INVENTORY\_вЂ‹ALLOCATIONS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  No inventory location found or enabled.

* INVOICE\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹PAID

  A payment has already been made for this invoice.

* MERCHANT\_вЂ‹ACCOUNT\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Merchant account configuration error.

* MERCHANT\_вЂ‹RULE

  The transaction was declined due to a merchant-defined rule.

* NON\_вЂ‹TEST\_вЂ‹ORDER\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹REACHED

  Non-test order limit reached. Use a test payment gateway to place another order.

* OFF\_вЂ‹SESSION\_вЂ‹REJECTED

  The off-session payment was rejected and requires customer action.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹DECLINED

  Payment method was declined by processor.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹INCOMPATIBLE\_вЂ‹WITH\_вЂ‹GATEWAY\_вЂ‹CONFIG

  Payment method cannot be used with the current payment gateway test mode configuration.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Payment method was not found.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SPECIFIED

  Payment method is not specified on subscription contract.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹UNSUPPORTED

  The payment method type is not supported.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹PROVIDER\_вЂ‹ERROR

  An exception occurred with the payment provider.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹PROVIDER\_вЂ‹IS\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ENABLED

  Payment provider is not enabled.

* PAYPAL\_вЂ‹ERROR\_вЂ‹GENERAL

  Paypal Error General.

* PROCESSING\_вЂ‹ERROR

  A processing error occurred with the payment provider.

* PURCHASE\_вЂ‹TYPE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORTED

  Purchase Type is not supported.

* RETRY\_вЂ‹DECLINED

  The payment retry was declined.

* TEST\_вЂ‹MODE

  Gateway is in test mode and attempted to bill a live payment method.

* TRANSACTION\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  The transaction exceeds the cardholder's limit.

* TRANSIENT\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Transient error, try again later.

* UNEXPECTED\_вЂ‹ERROR

  There was an unexpected error during the billing attempt.

***

### Fields

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹GenericвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionBillingAttemptGenericError#field-SubscriptionBillingAttemptGenericError.fields.code)

  OBJECT

  A base error type that applies to all uncategorized error classes.

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹InsufficientвЂ‹StockвЂ‹ProductвЂ‹VariantsвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionBillingAttemptInsufficientStockProductVariantsError#field-SubscriptionBillingAttemptInsufficientStockProductVariantsError.fields.code)

  An inventory error caused by an issue with one or more of the contract merchandise lines.

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹OutвЂ‹OfвЂ‹StockвЂ‹ProductвЂ‹VariantsвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionBillingAttemptOutOfStockProductVariantsError#field-SubscriptionBillingAttemptOutOfStockProductVariantsError.fields.code)

  OBJECT

  An inventory error caused by an issue with one or more of the contract merchandise lines.

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹ProcessingвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/interfaces/SubscriptionBillingAttemptProcessingError#fields-code)

  INTERFACE

  An error that prevented a billing attempt.

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹Attempt.errorCode](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionBillingAttempt#field-SubscriptionBillingAttempt.fields.errorCode)

  OBJECT

  Deprecated

***

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹InsufficientвЂ‹StockвЂ‹ProductвЂ‹VariantsвЂ‹Error.code](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionBillingAttemptInsufficientStockProductVariantsError#field-SubscriptionBillingAttemptInsufficientStockProductVariantsError.fields.code)

<a id="subscription-billing-attempt-general-error-code"></a>

## SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹GeneralвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Error codes for other billing attempt failures.

### Valid values

* CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹INVALID

  Customer is invalid.

* CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹FOUND

  Customer was not found.

* FREE\_вЂ‹GIFT\_вЂ‹CARD\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹ALLOWED

  Free gift cards are not allowed.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹BILLING\_вЂ‹ADDRESS

  Billing address is invalid.

* MERCHANT\_вЂ‹ACCOUNT\_вЂ‹ERROR

  Merchant account error.

* NON\_вЂ‹TEST\_вЂ‹ORDER\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹REACHED

  Non-test order limit reached.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹INCOMPATIBLE\_вЂ‹WITH\_вЂ‹GATEWAY\_вЂ‹CONFIG

  Payment method test mode incompatible with gateway.

<a id="subscription-billing-attempt-payment-error-code"></a>

## SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹PaymentвЂ‹ErrorвЂ‹Code

enum

Error codes for payment-related billing attempt failures.

### Valid values

* AMOUNT\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹LARGE

  The transaction amount is too large.

* AMOUNT\_вЂ‹TOO\_вЂ‹SMALL

  The transaction amount is too small.

* AUTHENTICATION\_вЂ‹ERROR

  There was an error during the authentication.

* AUTHENTICATION\_вЂ‹FAILED

  Authentication failed during the payment processing.

* AUTHENTICATION\_вЂ‹REQUIRED

  Additional authentication is required for this payment.

* BUYER\_вЂ‹CANCELED\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD

  The buyer canceled the payment method.

* CALL\_вЂ‹ISSUER

  The card issuer requires the cardholder to call.

* CANCELLED\_вЂ‹PAYMENT

  The payment was cancelled.

* CARD\_вЂ‹DECLINED

  The card was declined.

* CARD\_вЂ‹NUMBER\_вЂ‹INCORRECT

  The card number is incorrect.

* CONFIRMATION\_вЂ‹REJECTED

  The payment confirmation was rejected.

* DO\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹HONOR

  The card issuer returned 'do not honor'. The customer should reach out to their card-issuing bank for more information.

* EXPIRED\_вЂ‹BUYER\_вЂ‹ACTION

  The required buyer action has expired.

* EXPIRED\_вЂ‹CARD

  The card has expired.

* EXPIRED\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD

  Payment method has expired.

* FRAUD\_вЂ‹SUSPECTED

  Fraud was suspected on this transaction.

* GENERIC\_вЂ‹ERROR

  A generic payment error occurred.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹ADDRESS

  The billing address is incorrect.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹NUMBER

  The card number is incorrect.

* INCORRECT\_вЂ‹ZIP

  The postal/ZIP code is incorrect.

* INSUFFICIENT\_вЂ‹FUNDS

  The payment method has insufficient funds to complete the payment.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹CURRENCY

  The currency is not supported.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹CUSTOMER\_вЂ‹BILLING\_вЂ‹AGREEMENT

  The customer billing agreement is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹EXPIRY\_вЂ‹DATE

  The expiry date is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹NUMBER

  The card number is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD

  Payment method is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹PURCHASE\_вЂ‹TYPE

  The purchase type is invalid.

* INVALID\_вЂ‹SHIPPING\_вЂ‹ADDRESS

  The shipping address is invalid.

* INVOICE\_вЂ‹ALREADY\_вЂ‹PAID

  The invoice has already been paid.

* MERCHANT\_вЂ‹RULE

  The payment was declined due to a merchant rule.

* OFF\_вЂ‹SESSION\_вЂ‹REJECTED

  The off-session payment was rejected.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹DECLINED

  Payment method was declined.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD\_вЂ‹UNSUPPORTED

  The payment method type is not supported.

* PAYMENT\_вЂ‹PROVIDER\_вЂ‹ERROR

  An exception occurred with the payment provider.

* PAYPAL\_вЂ‹ERROR\_вЂ‹GENERAL

  A general PayPal error occurred.

* PROCESSING\_вЂ‹ERROR

  A processing error occurred.

* PURCHASE\_вЂ‹TYPE\_вЂ‹NOT\_вЂ‹SUPPORTED

  Purchase type is not supported.

* RETRY\_вЂ‹DECLINED

  The payment retry was declined.

* TEST\_вЂ‹MODE

  A test mode error occurred.

* TRANSACTION\_вЂ‹LIMIT\_вЂ‹EXCEEDED

  The transaction exceeded the limit.

* TRANSIENT\_вЂ‹ERROR

  A transient error occurred, try again later.

***

## SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹AttemptвЂ‹PaymentвЂ‹ProcessingвЂ‹Policy

enum

The payment processing policy for a billing attempt.

### Valid values

* FAIL\_вЂ‹UNLESS\_вЂ‹VALID\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹METHOD

  Attempt to process payment on the billing attempt and fail the billing attempt if the payment method is not valid or if a payment method does not exist.

* SKIP\_вЂ‹PAYMENT\_вЂ‹AND\_вЂ‹CREATE\_вЂ‹UNPAID\_вЂ‹ORDER

  Skip payment processing on the billing attempt and create an unpaid order.

enum

### Valid values

* CYCLE\_вЂ‹INDEX

  Sort by the `cycle_index` value.

* ID

  Sort by the `id` value.

***

### Fields

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹CycleвЂ‹EditedвЂ‹Contract.billingCycles(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionBillingCycleEditedContract#field-SubscriptionBillingCycleEditedContract.fields.billingCycles.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  Represents a subscription contract with billing cycles.

* [SubscriptionвЂ‹Draft.concatenatedBillingCycles(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionDraft#field-SubscriptionDraft.fields.concatenatedBillingCycles.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The `SubscriptionDraft` object represents a draft version of a [subscription contract](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/SubscriptionContract) before it's committed. It serves as a staging area for making changes to an existing subscription or creating a new one. The draft allows you to preview and modify various aspects of a subscription before applying the changes.

  Use the `SubscriptionDraft` object to:

  * Add, remove, or modify subscription lines and their quantities
  * Manage discounts (add, remove, or update manual and code-based discounts)
  * Configure delivery options and shipping methods
  * Set up billing and delivery policies
  * Manage customer payment methods
  * Add custom attributes and notes to generated orders
  * Configure billing cycles and next billing dates
  * Preview the projected state of the subscription

  Each `SubscriptionDraft` object maintains a projected state that shows how the subscription will look after the changes are committed. This allows you to preview the impact of your modifications before applying them. The draft can be associated with an existing subscription contract (for modifications) or used to create a new subscription.

  The draft remains in a draft state until it's committed, at which point the changes are applied to the subscription contract and the draft is no longer accessible.

  Learn more about [how subscription contracts work](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/contracts) and how to [build](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/contracts/build-a-subscription-contract), [update](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/contracts/update-a-subscription-contract), and [combine](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/purchase-options/subscriptions/contracts/combine-subscription-contracts) subscription contracts.

* [QueryвЂ‹Root.subscriptionBillingCycles(sortKey)](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/objects/QueryRoot#field-QueryRoot.fields.subscriptionBillingCycles.arguments.sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

  The schema's entry-point for queries. This acts as the public, top-level API from which all queries must start.

* [subscriptionвЂ‹BillingвЂ‹Cycles.sortKey](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-graphql/latest/queries/subscriptionBillingCycles#arguments-sortKey)

  ARGUMENT

***
